vehicles.name: Avisa
vehicles.meta_verdict: Mini-sub stored in the Kosatka; purpose-built for Cayo Perico preps.

vehicles.name: Dinghy
vehicles.meta_verdict: The basic Dinghy inflatable — special acquisition, unarmed, the foundation for the Weaponized variant.

vehicles.name: Jetmax
vehicles.meta_verdict: Jetmax speedboat at $299K — fast, sleek, and purely recreational; outgunned by every combat boat.

vehicles.name: Kosatka
vehicles.unlock_condition_note: Warstock listing unlocks after meeting Miguel Madrazo at The Music Locker (triggered automatically once player reaches sufficient GTA Online progression).
vehicles.meta_verdict: The best solo grind vehicle in the game, period.
vehicles.meta_note: Enables Cayo Perico Heist, the best solo money-maker at $1.5M+/hour; the single most important grinding purchase in the game.

vehicles.name: Kraken
vehicles.meta_verdict: The submersible submarine — more novelty than utility, but unforgettable to own.

vehicles.name: Kurtz 31 Patrol Boat
vehicles.meta_verdict: Fastest boat in the game — the benchmark for anything water-based.

vehicles.name: Longfin
vehicles.meta_verdict: Sleek speedboat with genuine racing pedigree; the Pariah of the water class.

vehicles.name: Marquis
vehicles.meta_verdict: The Marquis sailboat — GTA Online's most peaceful purchase; bring snacks, go nowhere fast.

vehicles.name: Police Predator
vehicles.meta_verdict: Fast police boat recently made purchasable; great for water patrol roleplay and racing.

vehicles.name: Seashark
vehicles.meta_verdict: Seashark jet ski at $16.9K — the cheapest fun you can have on GTA Online water.

vehicles.name: Speeder
vehicles.meta_verdict: Speeder racing boat at $325K — fast enough for water races, outclassed by the Longfin.

vehicles.name: Squalo
vehicles.meta_verdict: Squalo speedboat at $196.6K — competent water racer at a fair price, below the top tier.

vehicles.name: Submersible
vehicles.meta_verdict: The Submersible submarine — special acquisition for underwater exploration; peaceful, slow, fully submerged.

vehicles.name: Suntrap
vehicles.meta_verdict: Suntrap sailboat at $25K — the budget marina purchase; slower than the Marquis, cheaper than everything.

vehicles.name: Toro
vehicles.meta_verdict: Armed Toro speedboat at $1.75M — fast and capable in water combat, fragile under real fire.

vehicles.name: Tropic
vehicles.meta_verdict: Tropic leisure boat at $22K — the standard cabin cruiser; transport and vibes, no combat role.

vehicles.name: Tug
vehicles.meta_verdict: Harbor Tug at $1.25M — a working tugboat that GTA Online somehow lets you personally own.

vehicles.name: Weaponized Dinghy
vehicles.meta_verdict: Armed speedboat that holds its own in water-based missions; niche but reliable.
vehicles.meta_note: Armed boat. Very situational — only useful for specific water-based missions.

vehicles.name: Apocalypse Cerberus
vehicles.meta_verdict: Arena-only Cerberus truck with post-apoc weaponry — the heaviest Arena vehicle, locked behind Arena Points.

vehicles.name: Benson
vehicles.meta_verdict: The standard Benson box truck — unarmed, unarmored, the entry-level large delivery vehicle.

vehicles.name: Benson (Cluckin' Bell)
vehicles.meta_verdict: Cluckin' Bell-branded Benson at $685K — the most expensive chicken delivery truck in gaming history.

vehicles.name: Biff
vehicles.meta_verdict: The Biff heavy truck — unarmed commercial hauler with special acquisition and zero competitive use.

vehicles.name: Bobcat Security Stockade
vehicles.meta_verdict: Armored security truck at $2.44M — bulletproof cash transport that you can actually buy for yourself.

vehicles.name: Future Shock Cerberus
vehicles.meta_verdict: Futuristic Arena Cerberus — sci-fi heavy truck with weaponry, locked behind the Arena ecosystem.

vehicles.name: Hauler
vehicles.meta_verdict: The base Hauler semi — the stepping stone to the Custom; buy the Custom instead.

vehicles.name: Hauler Custom
vehicles.meta_verdict: Same as Phantom Custom — a crew vehicle masquerading as a solo one.
vehicles.meta_note: Used in certain Gunrunning sell missions. Situationally useful.

vehicles.name: Mule
vehicles.meta_verdict: Mule box truck at $27K — GTA Online's most budget-friendly hauler, priced exactly right.

vehicles.name: Mule Custom
vehicles.meta_verdict: Large crate delivery truck; needed for big Import/Export jobs, nothing more.
vehicles.meta_note: Used in MC and Bunker sell missions. If you run those businesses, you'll use this.

vehicles.name: Nightmare Cerberus
vehicles.meta_verdict: Nightmare Arena Cerberus — bone-clad heavy truck that never leaves the Arena War circuit.

vehicles.name: Packer
vehicles.meta_verdict: The Packer car transporter — special acquisition, used in stunt setups and vehicle transport missions.

vehicles.name: Phantom
vehicles.meta_verdict: The base Phantom semi — the stepping stone to the Custom and Wedge; buy those instead.

vehicles.name: Phantom Custom
vehicles.meta_verdict: Large bunker sale vehicle; painful solo, worth owning in a grinding crew.
vehicles.meta_note: Required for Phantom Wedge sell missions. Situationally essential.

vehicles.name: Phantom Wedge
vehicles.meta_verdict: The Phantom Wedge at $2.554M — a plow-equipped semi built to destroy other vehicles on contact.

vehicles.name: Pounder
vehicles.meta_verdict: Base Pounder heavy truck — special acquisition for the Custom upgrade path.

vehicles.name: Pounder Custom
vehicles.meta_verdict: Third large bunker sale vehicle; learn the route or it will cost you.
vehicles.meta_note: Used in MC sell missions. Situationally useful for large-stock sales.

vehicles.name: Stockade
vehicles.meta_verdict: Armored Stockade at $2.24M — bulletproof commercial truck that absorbs more punishment than most military vehicles.

vehicles.name: Terrorbyte
vehicles.unlock_condition_note: Requires owning a Nightclub. Warstock listing does not appear without one.
vehicles.meta_verdict: Useless without the Oppressor Mk II; completely indispensable with it.
vehicles.meta_note: Enables Client Jobs and Oppressor Mk II weapons/boost upgrade. Central hub for efficient grinding operations.

vehicles.name: Apocalypse Issi
vehicles.meta_verdict: Arena-only Issi with post-apoc weaponry — the world's deadliest Mini, locked behind Arena Points.

vehicles.name: Asbo
vehicles.meta_verdict: E-tier Asbo — British chav car aesthetic transplanted to GTA; fun character, limited ceiling.

vehicles.name: Blista
vehicles.meta_verdict: D-tier base Blista with no listed price — the Kanjo's lesser ancestor, acquirable but outclassed.

vehicles.name: Blista Kanjo
vehicles.meta_verdict: B-tier Blista Kanjo — Honda Civic-style hatchback that competes seriously near the top of the class.

vehicles.name: Brioso 300
vehicles.meta_verdict: G-tier Brioso 300 — buy the R/A at $155K instead; it's A-tier and costs less.

vehicles.name: Brioso 300 Widebody
vehicles.meta_verdict: E-tier Brioso 300 with the wide kit — same result as the base, more visual attitude.

vehicles.name: Brioso R/A
vehicles.meta_verdict: A-tier Brioso R/A at $155K — best value compact racer in the game, full stop.

vehicles.name: Club
vehicles.meta_verdict: S-tier Club at $1.28M — the undisputed king of Compacts; nothing in the class touches it.

vehicles.name: Dilettante
vehicles.meta_verdict: Removed SAM 2023. G-tier Dilettante — GTA Online's hybrid car joke, taken by the SAM cull.

vehicles.name: Future Shock Issi
vehicles.meta_verdict: Futuristic Arena Issi — sci-fi compact with weaponry, entirely gated behind the Arena ecosystem.

vehicles.name: Issi
vehicles.meta_verdict: Removed SAM 2023. E-tier Issi — the Mini Cooper tribute taken by the SAM cull.

vehicles.name: Issi Classic
vehicles.meta_verdict: F-tier Issi Classic — retro Mini tribute that looks the part and races near the bottom.

vehicles.name: Nightmare Issi
vehicles.meta_verdict: Nightmare Arena Issi — bone-decorated Mini that murders things exclusively inside Arena War.

vehicles.name: Panto
vehicles.meta_verdict: C-tier Panto at $85K — GTA's tiniest car; chaotic, endearing, and surprisingly competitive.

vehicles.name: Prairie
vehicles.meta_verdict: Removed SAM 2023. E-tier Prairie — compact people-mover that left no impression before or after removal.

vehicles.name: Rhapsody
vehicles.meta_verdict: C-tier Rhapsody — retro compact with genuine racing credentials and a fair price.

vehicles.name: Weevil
vehicles.meta_verdict: D-tier base Weevil — the VW Bug tribute whose Custom variant in Muscle class is far superior.

vehicles.name: Cognoscenti Cabrio
vehicles.meta_verdict: Removed SAM 2023. E-tier Cognoscenti Cabrio — open-top luxury coupe gone with the update.

vehicles.name: Euros X32
vehicles.meta_verdict: C-tier Euros X32 at $1.5M — Porsche 911-style coupe that races mid-pack without distinction.

vehicles.name: Exemplar
vehicles.meta_verdict: Removed SAM 2023. C-tier Exemplar — sleek luxury coupe taken by the SAM cull.

vehicles.name: F620
vehicles.meta_verdict: Removed SAM 2023. E-tier F620 — forgettable luxury coupe that left no legacy behind.

vehicles.name: Felon
vehicles.meta_verdict: Removed SAM 2023. B-tier Felon — luxury coupe from GTA Online's early era, gone with the update.

vehicles.name: Felon GT
vehicles.meta_verdict: Removed SAM 2023. F-tier Felon GT — the GT variant of the Felon, equally removed.

vehicles.name: FR36
vehicles.meta_verdict: S-tier Coupe that outpaces everything in its class — the FR36 sets the benchmark.

vehicles.name: Jackal
vehicles.meta_verdict: Removed SAM 2023. C-tier Jackal — budget coupe from GTA Online's early era, quietly removed.

vehicles.name: Kanjo SJ
vehicles.meta_verdict: G-tier Kanjo SJ — Civic-inspired tuner coupe that races below what its street pedigree suggests.

vehicles.name: Oracle
vehicles.meta_verdict: Removed SAM 2023. F-tier Oracle — luxury coupe that never rose above mid-lower tier before removal.

vehicles.name: Oracle XS
vehicles.meta_verdict: Removed SAM 2023. G-tier Oracle XS — the sportier Oracle variant, equally gone.

vehicles.name: Postlude
vehicles.meta_verdict: H-tier Postlude at $1.31M — overpriced for H-tier; buy it purely for the garage aesthetic.

vehicles.name: Previon
vehicles.meta_verdict: A-tier Previon — the strongest competition for the FR36 at the top of the Coupe class.

vehicles.name: Sentinel
vehicles.meta_verdict: Removed SAM 2023. F-tier Sentinel — GTA Online's BMW tribute, retired with the SAM update.

vehicles.name: Sentinel XS
vehicles.meta_verdict: F-tier Sentinel XS at $60K — the only surviving Sentinel variant; cheap enough to own casually.

vehicles.name: Windsor
vehicles.meta_verdict: G-tier Windsor — stunning Rolls-Royce-style coupe that belongs in a penthouse garage, not on a circuit.

vehicles.name: Windsor Drop
vehicles.meta_verdict: G-tier Windsor Drop — the convertible Windsor; same beauty, same G-tier reality.

vehicles.name: Zion
vehicles.meta_verdict: Removed SAM 2023. B-tier Zion — the Maserati-style coupe taken by the SAM cull.

vehicles.name: Zion Cabrio
vehicles.meta_verdict: Removed SAM 2023. B-tier Zion Cabrio — convertible Zion variant gone alongside the hardtop.

vehicles.name: BMX
vehicles.meta_verdict: Street-culture staple; the original GTA bicycle, cheap tricks and cheaper price.

vehicles.name: Cruiser
vehicles.meta_verdict: Laid-back beach cruiser; the most relaxed vehicle in Los Santos.

vehicles.name: Endurex Race Bike
vehicles.meta_verdict: Road racing bicycle for players who insist on the authentic cycling experience.

vehicles.name: Fixter
vehicles.meta_verdict: Fixed-gear hipster bike; style-forward, gear-backward.

vehicles.name: Inductor
vehicles.meta_verdict: Mountain bike built for off-road; the only CYCLE that pretends to have terrain ambitions.

vehicles.name: Junk Energy Inductor
vehicles.meta_verdict: Branded Inductor with energy drink livery; same mountain bike, corporate sponsorship included.

vehicles.name: Scorcher
vehicles.meta_verdict: BMX-style trick bike; lighter and more nimble than the standard BMX.

vehicles.name: Tri-Cycles Race Bike
vehicles.meta_verdict: Racing bicycle in full kit; for the GTA player who takes the Tour de Los Santos seriously.

vehicles.name: Whippet Race Bike
vehicles.meta_verdict: The fastest bicycle in GTA Online — which is not a compliment in a world full of supercars.

vehicles.name: Ambulance
vehicles.meta_verdict: Paramedic flex; saves lives more than it costs money, usually.

vehicles.name: Buffalo Cruiser
vehicles.meta_verdict: Armored police variant of the Buffalo; protection over firepower.

vehicles.name: Buffalo STX Pursuit
vehicles.meta_verdict: S-tier performance locked behind a $5.37M cop livery — buy the base STX for racing, this for roleplay.

vehicles.name: Burrito (Bail Enforcement)
vehicles.unlock_condition_note: Bundled free with any Bail Office purchase (Maze Bank Foreclosures). Not sold separately.
vehicles.meta_verdict: Bail enforcement van; niche roleplay for bounty hunters.

vehicles.name: Caracara Pursuit
vehicles.unlock_condition_note: Straight Warstock purchase, no property gate. Owning one enables Dispatch Work missions. Trade price ($4,001,250) unlocks after completing "Slush Fund" as mission leader AND "Dispatch Work - Obstruction of Justice".
vehicles.meta_verdict: Caracara in cop paint at $5.335M — performance-identical to the base at triple the price.

vehicles.name: Coquette D10 Pursuit
vehicles.meta_verdict: E-tier pursuit at $5.62M — GTA Online's most expensive flex for performance that doesn't warrant it.

vehicles.name: Dominator FX Interceptor
vehicles.meta_verdict: Dominator FX in interceptor livery at $4.95M — S-tier muscle in cop paint for players who need both.

vehicles.name: Dorado Cruiser
vehicles.meta_verdict: D-tier Dorado in patrol livery at $5M — the least justifiable pursuit purchase in the class.

vehicles.name: FIB
vehicles.meta_verdict: The FIB federal vehicle — special acquisition only, government aesthetic without the government clearance.

vehicles.name: Fire Truck
vehicles.meta_verdict: Big red truck for roleplayers and firefighting enthusiasts; zero combat utility.

vehicles.name: Gauntlet Interceptor
vehicles.meta_verdict: Gauntlet Hellfire-spec in interceptor livery at $5.42M — C-tier muscle, top-tier cop aesthetic.

vehicles.name: Greenwood Cruiser
vehicles.meta_verdict: Imani Tech Greenwood in cruiser livery — the gadgets survive the cop paint job at $4.91M.

vehicles.name: Impaler LX Cruiser
vehicles.meta_verdict: C-tier Impaler LX in cruiser livery at $4.8M — lowrider muscle dressed as law enforcement.

vehicles.name: Impaler SZ Cruiser
vehicles.meta_verdict: B-tier Impaler SZ in cruiser livery at $4.72M — the best-performing pursuit variant in the class.

vehicles.name: Lifeguard
vehicles.meta_verdict: Beach patrol truck; pure novelty and summer vibes.

vehicles.name: Outreach Faction
vehicles.meta_verdict: Outreach Faction at $4.15M — community services livery on a lowrider; niche collector purchase.

vehicles.name: Park Ranger
vehicles.meta_verdict: Park Ranger at $2.98M — Blaine County Parks livery for players who take their roleplay seriously.

vehicles.name: Police Bike
vehicles.meta_verdict: Two-wheeled patrol vehicle; fragile but fun for cop roleplay.

vehicles.name: Police Cruiser
vehicles.meta_verdict: The classic LSPD cruiser — acquirable through specific methods, collector and roleplay value only.

vehicles.name: Police Prison Bus
vehicles.meta_verdict: Transport for criminals; more roleplay than actual function.

vehicles.name: Police Rancher
vehicles.meta_verdict: Police Rancher SUV — law enforcement throwback with special acquisition only.

vehicles.name: Police Riot
vehicles.meta_verdict: Armored riot control vehicle; slow, heavy, intimidating.

vehicles.name: Police Roadcruiser
vehicles.meta_verdict: Police Roadcruiser — the marked police sedan with no direct purchase path; special acquisition only.

vehicles.name: Police Transporter
vehicles.meta_verdict: Police Transporter — prisoner transport van with no direct purchase; roleplay and content creation territory.

vehicles.name: RCV
vehicles.meta_verdict: Riot control vehicle; defensive rather than offensive firepower.

vehicles.name: Sheriff Cruiser
vehicles.meta_verdict: Sheriff's Department cruiser — special acquisition, zero competitive use, full roleplay value.

vehicles.name: Sheriff SUV
vehicles.meta_verdict: Sheriff SUV — law enforcement off-road vehicle with special acquisition only.

vehicles.name: Stanier LE Cruiser
vehicles.meta_verdict: E-tier Stanier LE in cruiser livery — a SAM-removed sedan's only surviving purchase option, at $4.69M.

vehicles.name: Terminus Patrol
vehicles.unlock_condition_note: Straight Warstock purchase. Trade price ($3,843,750) unlocks after completing "Slush Fund" as leader AND "Dispatch Work - Health Code Violations".
vehicles.meta_verdict: A-tier Terminus in patrol livery at $5.125M — best-performing pursuit vehicle, still purely collector territory.

vehicles.name: Unmarked Cruiser
vehicles.meta_verdict: Unmarked Cruiser — the undercover cop car of GTA Online; special acquisition, real collector appeal.

vehicles.name: Akula
vehicles.unlock_condition_note: Requires both a Facility AND a Hangar to purchase and store. Trade price ($3,375,000) unlocks after completing the "Setup: Server Farm" mission as Doomsday Heist leader.
vehicles.meta_verdict: Goes off-radar; that one feature is worth every penny of the price.
vehicles.meta_note: Off-radar capability makes it excellent for avoiding griefers during sell missions. Strong utility.

vehicles.name: Annihilator
vehicles.meta_verdict: The base Annihilator at $1.825M — unarmed heavy helicopter completely obsoleted by the Stealth variant.

vehicles.name: Annihilator Stealth
vehicles.unlock_condition_note: Requires a Hangar for storage. Trade price ($2,902,500) unlocks after completing The Cayo Perico Heist as leader using this vehicle as the approach.
vehicles.meta_verdict: The Akula's less agile cousin; stealth capable but costs $3.87M for the privilege.
vehicles.meta_note: Off-radar helicopter. Decent but outclassed by Akula for stealth operations.

vehicles.name: Buzzard
vehicles.meta_verdict: The missile-less Buzzard; fine for fast travel, not for combat.
vehicles.meta_note: Useful attack helicopter but no personal spawn perk without CEO. Step down from Buzzard Attack Chopper.

vehicles.name: Buzzard Attack Chopper
vehicles.meta_verdict: Seven years later, still the best helicopter for making money.
vehicles.meta_note: Best budget grind vehicle before Oppressor Mk II. Fast, weaponized, spawns near you as CEO. Excellent early-game.

vehicles.name: Cargobob
vehicles.meta_verdict: Import/Export large vehicles without this is a nightmare; with it, easy money.
vehicles.meta_note: Required for certain sell missions (vehicle cargo). Invaluable when needed.

vehicles.name: Conada
vehicles.meta_verdict: Military reconnaissance helicopter at $2.45M — light scout heli with limited but real combat capability.

vehicles.name: DH-7 Iron Mule
vehicles.meta_verdict: Heavy transport helicopter at $2.345M — cargo-capable lifter, though the Cargobob remains the standard for lifting.

vehicles.name: FH-1 Hunter
vehicles.unlock_condition_note: Requires owning a Hangar before Warstock listing appears. Trade price ($3,100,000) unlocks after completing 42 Air Freight Cargo source missions as MC President.
vehicles.meta_verdict: The best attack helicopter in the game if helicopters are your thing.
vehicles.meta_note: Military attack helicopter. Powerful but high skill ceiling and expensive.

vehicles.name: Frogger
vehicles.meta_verdict: The Frogger civilian helicopter at $1.3M — basic unarmed transport; gets the job done quietly.

vehicles.name: Havok
vehicles.meta_verdict: Havok light attack heli at $2.3M — nimble and capable, but outgunned by every heavy combat helicopter.

vehicles.name: Higgins Helitours Maverick
vehicles.meta_verdict: Helitours tour helicopter — special acquisition with a sightseeing livery; unarmed, civilian, charming.

vehicles.name: Maverick
vehicles.meta_verdict: The Maverick at $780K — the original GTA helicopter; simple, reliable, and evergreen for basic transport.

vehicles.name: Police Maverick
vehicles.meta_verdict: Police-liveried Maverick with special acquisition — same performance as the base, cop aesthetic only.

vehicles.name: Savage
vehicles.meta_verdict: Big attack helicopter that costs more and performs worse than the Buzzard Attack Chopper.
vehicles.meta_note: Powerful attack helicopter but too expensive for what it offers. Better options exist at lower cost.

vehicles.name: Sea Sparrow
vehicles.meta_verdict: Water-landing helicopter; the Sparrow for players without a Kosatka.

vehicles.name: Skylift
vehicles.meta_verdict: The Skylift heavy-lift helicopter — special acquisition only; oversized vehicle transport when the Cargobob won't do.

vehicles.name: Sparrow
vehicles.meta_verdict: The Kosatka's essential companion — buy it the same session.
vehicles.meta_note: Required for Cayo Perico scope-out and fast travel within the Kosatka. Unlocks as part of the Kosatka package.

vehicles.name: SuperVolito
vehicles.meta_verdict: SuperVolito luxury helicopter at $2.113M — civilian executive transport with zero combat utility.

vehicles.name: SuperVolito Carbon
vehicles.meta_verdict: SuperVolito Carbon — the most expensive way to transport yourself without weapons or practical upgrades.

vehicles.name: Swift
vehicles.meta_verdict: Swift executive helicopter at $1.5M — luxury transport with a comfortable price and zero combat use.

vehicles.name: Swift Deluxe
vehicles.meta_verdict: Swift Deluxe at $5.15M — the ultimate luxury helicopter flex; the price is the entire point.

vehicles.name: Valkyrie
vehicles.meta_verdict: The old meta helicopter gunship — still fearsome, now mostly outclassed.
vehicles.meta_note: Attack helicopter with side gunners. Good for co-op but outclassed by Akula solo.

vehicles.name: Valkyrie MOD.0
vehicles.meta_verdict: Valkyrie MOD.0 — the modified Valkyrie variant with special acquisition and enhanced weaponry over the base.

vehicles.name: Volatus
vehicles.meta_verdict: Volatus luxury helicopter at $2.295M — executive transport slotting between the Swift and SuperVolito.

vehicles.name: Weaponized Conada
vehicles.meta_verdict: Weaponized Conada at $3.385M — the armed Conada variant with proper military firepower added.

vehicles.name: Custom Flatbed
vehicles.meta_verdict: Custom Flatbed — industrial hauler for missions and content creation; no competitive use.

vehicles.name: Cutter
vehicles.meta_verdict: The Cutter road-paving machine — exists for completionists and content creators only.

vehicles.name: Dock Handler
vehicles.meta_verdict: Dock Handler port vehicle — special acquisition, zero competitive or grinding relevance.

vehicles.name: Dozer
vehicles.meta_verdict: The Dozer bulldozer — a GTA Online tradition since the original game; still just a bulldozer.

vehicles.name: Dump
vehicles.meta_verdict: Dump mining truck at $1M — GTA Online's most massive road vehicle, impossible to ignore.

vehicles.name: Flatbed
vehicles.meta_verdict: The Flatbed truck — heavy transport for missions and heist setups; special acquisition.

vehicles.name: Guardian
vehicles.meta_verdict: Guardian armored truck at $375K — security vehicle for the completionist; no combat or racing application.

vehicles.name: Mixer
vehicles.meta_verdict: The Mixer cement truck — construction vehicle for content creators and mission runners.

vehicles.name: Rubble
vehicles.meta_verdict: The Rubble garbage truck — industrial class entry for collectors who want every vehicle ticked.

vehicles.name: Tipper
vehicles.meta_verdict: The Tipper dump truck — heavy industrial hauler with zero practical GTA Online application beyond owning it.

vehicles.name: Anti-Aircraft Trailer
vehicles.meta_verdict: Towed AA missile battery at $1.862M — effective anti-air when parked, helpless without a tow vehicle.

vehicles.name: APC
vehicles.unlock_condition_note: Purchase unrestricted from Warstock. Trade price ($2,325,000) unlocks after completing "Offshore Assets" Mobile Operations mission as leader.
vehicles.meta_verdict: Amphibious armored cannon; surprisingly capable, too slow for serious grinding.
vehicles.meta_note: Armored personnel carrier. Useful but slow and expensive for what it offers.

vehicles.name: Apocalypse Scarab
vehicles.meta_verdict: Arena-only Scarab with post-apoc armaments — compact tracked Arena vehicle locked behind Arena Points.

vehicles.name: Barracks
vehicles.meta_verdict: Military troop transport at $450K — unarmed personnel carrier with zero combat or racing relevance.

vehicles.name: Barracks Semi
vehicles.meta_verdict: Semi-truck variant of the Barracks — larger, slower, equally unarmed and equally niche.

vehicles.name: Barrage
vehicles.meta_verdict: Military buggy with weapons that excels at controlled chaos across any terrain.

vehicles.name: Chernobog
vehicles.unlock_condition_note: Requires owning a Facility before Warstock listing appears. Trade price ($1,125,000) unlocks after completing "Escort ULP" setup as heist leader.
vehicles.meta_verdict: Static missile launcher needing a Facility; zero grinding applications, zero excuses.
vehicles.meta_note: Massive missile launcher. Fun but impractical; too slow and unwieldy for real grind use.

vehicles.name: Crusader
vehicles.meta_verdict: Military open-top jeep at $225K — unarmed and basic; the cheapest purpose-built military vehicle available.

vehicles.name: Future Shock Scarab
vehicles.meta_verdict: Futuristic Arena Scarab — sci-fi tracked combat vehicle locked entirely in the Arena ecosystem.

vehicles.name: Half-track
vehicles.meta_verdict: Armored quad-cannon truck; decent for sales, no pretense of being subtle.
vehicles.meta_note: Heavily armored with turret. Good for sell missions with a crew.

vehicles.name: Invade and Persuade Tank
vehicles.unlock_condition_note: Requires owning an Arcade property.
vehicles.meta_verdict: Fun RC toy; the Arcade is worth owning, but not for this specifically.
vehicles.meta_note: RC toy tank. No practical grinding value whatsoever.

vehicles.name: Nightmare Scarab
vehicles.meta_verdict: Nightmare Arena Scarab — bone-decorated tracked Arena vehicle for players deep in the Arena lifestyle.

vehicles.name: Rhino Tank
vehicles.meta_verdict: The classic GTA tank — slow, durable, still the most recognizable vehicle in the game.
vehicles.meta_note: Classic tank. Great for chaos, poor for efficiency. Fun but not a grind staple.

vehicles.name: Thruster
vehicles.unlock_condition_note: Requires owning a Facility. Trade price ($1,875,000) unlocks after completing The Doomsday Scenario as Heist Leader (final act of Doomsday Heist).
vehicles.meta_verdict: Jetpack with missiles — the most fun Facility vehicle, the least practical.
vehicles.meta_note: Jetpack with weapons. Niche utility, expensive to unlock, outclassed by Oppressor Mk II.

vehicles.name: TM-02 Khanjali
vehicles.unlock_condition_note: Requires owning a Facility before Warstock listing appears. Trade price ($2,895,000) unlocks after completing the "Khanjali" setup mission as heist leader.
vehicles.meta_verdict: Facility-gated tank that contributes nothing to any income loop.
vehicles.meta_note: Futuristic tank. Slow and cumbersome. Outclassed for grinding; mainly a fun/novelty vehicle.

vehicles.name: Vetir
vehicles.meta_verdict: Vetir armored personnel carrier at $1.63M — heavily armored transport that sacrifices everything for protection.

vehicles.name: Akuma
vehicles.meta_verdict: Removed SAM 2023. Fast sport bike in its era — outpaced by every modern motorcycle before removal.

vehicles.name: Apocalypse Deathbike
vehicles.meta_verdict: Arena-only Deathbike with post-apoc weaponry — the most feared Arena motorcycle variant.

vehicles.name: Avarus
vehicles.meta_verdict: Removed SAM 2023. Biker DLC chopper — solid custom aesthetic, gone with the update.

vehicles.name: Bagger
vehicles.meta_verdict: Removed SAM 2023. Touring cruiser with zero racing intent — correctly filed under removed.

vehicles.name: Bati 801
vehicles.meta_verdict: The slower Bati; useful for setups, strictly second to the 801RR.
vehicles.meta_note: Fast budget motorcycle for missions. Good early-game option.

vehicles.name: Bati 801RR
vehicles.meta_verdict: Removed from stores June 2023 — if you have it, it's still elite for setups.
vehicles.meta_note: Fast motorcycle for contact missions. Good early-game option before Oppressor Mk II.

vehicles.name: BF400
vehicles.meta_verdict: The BF400 at $95K is the go-to off-road motorcycle — cheap, capable, and universally used.

vehicles.name: Carbon RS
stores: Grand Theft Auto V and Online: Expanded and Enhanced Edition
vehicles.meta_verdict: The fastest non-drag motorcycle available — if you're racing on two wheels, this is it.

vehicles.name: Chimera
vehicles.meta_verdict: Scrambler-style bike at $210K — capable off-road, forgettable on pavement.

vehicles.name: Cliffhanger
vehicles.meta_verdict: Removed SAM 2023. Dirt bike that carved its niche — off-road capable, store listing gone.

vehicles.name: Daemon
vehicles.meta_verdict: Removed SAM 2023. Old chopper with no listed price — likely mission-locked, now doubly inaccessible.

vehicles.name: Daemon Custom
vehicles.meta_verdict: Biker DLC custom chopper — pure outlaw aesthetic with zero competitive racing application.

vehicles.name: Defiler
vehicles.meta_verdict: Big bagger-style touring bike at $412K — aggressive look, underwhelming pace.

vehicles.name: Diabolus
vehicles.meta_verdict: Base Diabolus electric bike — buy the Custom version instead; this is just the foundation.

vehicles.name: Diabolus Custom
vehicles.meta_verdict: Electric Diabolus Custom with boost ability — fun novelty bike with a genuine trick up its sleeve.

vehicles.name: Double-T
vehicles.meta_verdict: Removed SAM 2023. Low-tier sport bike from GTA Online's early era — long forgotten.

vehicles.name: Enduro
vehicles.meta_verdict: Removed SAM 2023. Dirt bike that predated the BF400 — the BF400 made it irrelevant first.

vehicles.name: Esskey
vehicles.meta_verdict: Removed SAM 2023. Electric delivery scooter at $264K — a novelty purchase that nobody needed.

vehicles.name: Faggio
vehicles.meta_verdict: Removed SAM 2023. GTA's iconic moped — slow, beloved, appropriately retired.

vehicles.name: Faggio Mod
vehicles.meta_verdict: Removed SAM 2023. Modded Faggio moped — slightly faster, equally unmissable.

vehicles.name: Faggio Sport
vehicles.meta_verdict: Removed SAM 2023. The fastest moped in GTA history — that's the ceiling, and it was enough.

vehicles.name: FCR 1000
vehicles.meta_verdict: Base FCR 1000 — buy the Custom version instead; this is just the stepping stone.

vehicles.name: FCR 1000 Custom
vehicles.meta_verdict: The best-looking cruiser in the game with appropriately slow lap times — buy it for the garage.

vehicles.name: Future Shock Deathbike
vehicles.meta_verdict: Futuristic Arena Deathbike — sci-fi armored motorcycle locked behind Arena buy-in.

vehicles.name: Gargoyle
vehicles.meta_verdict: Off-road dirt bike that rivals the BF400 — capable alternative at a similar price point.

vehicles.name: Hakuchou
vehicles.meta_verdict: Removed SAM 2023. The base before the Drag — solid sport bike power-crept by its own Custom version.

vehicles.name: Hakuchou Drag
vehicles.meta_verdict: The drag racing king — and more competitive in standard races than it looks.

vehicles.name: Hexer
vehicles.meta_verdict: Removed SAM 2023. Biker DLC chopper — Lost MC aesthetic, taken by the SAM cull.

vehicles.name: Innovation
vehicles.meta_verdict: Removed SAM 2023. Old cruiser that never innovated enough — the name wrote the review.

vehicles.name: Lectro
vehicles.meta_verdict: Electric sport bike at $997.5K — fast for its era, outpaced by newer additions.

vehicles.name: Manchez
vehicles.meta_verdict: Base Manchez at $67K — the stepping stone to the Scout, not a destination itself.

vehicles.name: Manchez Scout
vehicles.meta_verdict: The original scout bike; solid for off-road setups before the Acid Lab era.
vehicles.meta_note: Basic off-road bike. Cheap option for wilderness missions.

vehicles.name: Manchez Scout C
vehicles.unlock_condition_note: Free — bundled with the Brickade 6x6 once purchased and upgraded at The Freakshop. Same First Dose + Last Dose gate.
vehicles.meta_verdict: Free with the Brickade 6x6 and genuinely earns its keep in Acid Lab preps.
vehicles.meta_note: Used in MC sell missions. Cheap and functional for that specific use case.

vehicles.name: Nemesis
vehicles.meta_verdict: Removed SAM 2023. Low-spec sport bike from the early era — long outclassed and now gone.

vehicles.name: Nightblade
vehicles.meta_verdict: Dark chopper with a solid aesthetic — doesn't race and doesn't pretend to.

vehicles.name: Nightmare Deathbike
vehicles.meta_verdict: Nightmare Arena Deathbike — bone-decorated monster motorcycle that never leaves the Arena circuit.

vehicles.name: Oppressor
vehicles.meta_verdict: The original hoverboard — fun relic, completely outclassed by the Mk II.
vehicles.meta_note: Mk I rocket bike; useful but requires Mk II for full potential. Decent mid-tier grind option.

vehicles.name: Oppressor Mk II
vehicles.unlock_condition_note: Purchase unrestricted from Warstock, but weapons and boost require a Terrorbyte with Mobile Workshop. Functionally limited without it.
vehicles.meta_verdict: Buy this before anything else; it makes every other activity faster.
vehicles.meta_note: Best all-around grind vehicle: fast travel, weaponized, griefing defense. Essential for efficient CEO/MC work.

vehicles.name: PCJ 600
vehicles.meta_verdict: Removed SAM 2023. GTA's OG stunt bike — legendary in story mode, barely relevant in Online.

vehicles.name: Pizza Boy
vehicles.meta_verdict: Delivery moped at $195K — GTA Online's most expensive joke purchase.

vehicles.name: Powersurge
vehicles.meta_verdict: Electric sport bike with genuine racing pedigree — strong alternative to the Carbon RS.

vehicles.name: Rampant Rocket
vehicles.meta_verdict: Rocket-style sport bike at $925K — fast, distinctive, and competitively relevant in motorcycle racing.

vehicles.name: Rat Bike
vehicles.meta_verdict: Removed SAM 2023. Junkyard aesthetic chopper — more character than performance, correctly removed.

vehicles.name: Reever
vehicles.meta_verdict: Pricey sport bike at $1.9M that sits just below the Carbon RS — fast but not the fastest.

vehicles.name: Ruffian
vehicles.meta_verdict: Removed SAM 2023. Budget sport bike that never threatened the top spots — quietly removed.

vehicles.name: Sanchez
vehicles.meta_verdict: Removed SAM 2023. The original dirt bike — a GTA icon retired by the update.

vehicles.name: Sanchez (livery)
vehicles.meta_verdict: Livery Sanchez at $7K — the OG dirt bike's purchasable survivor, charmingly slow.

vehicles.name: Sanctus
vehicles.meta_verdict: Removed SAM 2023. Skull-decorated chopper at $1.995M — wildly overpriced even before removal.

vehicles.name: Shinobi
vehicles.meta_verdict: Newer sport bike with top-tier speed — sleek Dinka design that competes with Carbon RS.

vehicles.name: Shotaro
vehicles.meta_verdict: Tron-inspired light cycle that's genuinely fast — best-looking bike with real racing credentials.

vehicles.name: Sovereign
vehicles.meta_verdict: Removed SAM 2023. Harley-style cruiser — pure aesthetics, no competitive legacy to miss.

vehicles.name: Stryder
vehicles.meta_verdict: Futuristic electric dirt bike at $670K — genuinely capable off-road with a premium price tag.

vehicles.name: Thrust
vehicles.meta_verdict: Removed SAM 2023. Sport bike with decent speed — removed before it could build a following.

vehicles.name: Vader
vehicles.meta_verdict: Removed SAM 2023. Budget commuter bike from GTA Online's early days — gone, rarely mourned.

vehicles.name: Vindicator
vehicles.meta_verdict: Heavyweight cruiser at $630K — buy it for the presence, not the lap times.

vehicles.name: Vortex
vehicles.meta_verdict: Futuristic Pegassi concept bike at $356K — striking design that doesn't translate to meaningful performance.

vehicles.name: Wolfsbane
vehicles.meta_verdict: Removed SAM 2023. Biker DLC chopper — outlaw aesthetic the SAM cull took quietly.

vehicles.name: Zombie Bobber
vehicles.meta_verdict: Removed SAM 2023. Biker DLC bobber — stylish zombie aesthetic, average performance, now gone.

vehicles.name: Zombie Chopper
vehicles.meta_verdict: The Zombie Chopper survived SAM while its Bobber sibling didn't — both are equally slow.

vehicles.name: Apocalypse Dominator
vehicles.meta_verdict: Arena-only Dominator with post-apoc weaponry — fully gated behind Arena Points and the Workshop.

vehicles.name: Apocalypse Impaler
vehicles.meta_verdict: Arena-only Impaler with wrecking attachments — Mad Max energy locked behind Arena buy-in.

vehicles.name: Apocalypse Imperator
vehicles.meta_verdict: Arena-only Imperator — the most visually aggressive Arena muscle, appropriately expensive to unlock.

vehicles.name: Apocalypse Slamvan
vehicles.meta_verdict: Arena-only weaponized Slamvan — junkyard monster you'll never see outside Arena War events.

vehicles.name: Arbiter GT
vehicles.meta_verdict: E-tier muscle coupe at $1.58M — looks great in the garage, races without distinction.

vehicles.name: Beater Dukes
vehicles.meta_verdict: E-tier junkyard Dukes with a weathered look — charming rust bucket with minimal pace.

vehicles.name: Blade
vehicles.meta_verdict: Removed SAM 2023. D-tier classic chopper — cult following couldn't save it from the cull.

vehicles.name: Brigham
vehicles.meta_verdict: J-tier Lampadati luxury coupe lost in the wrong vehicle class — races like it knows it.

vehicles.name: Broadway
vehicles.meta_verdict: K-tier stretched luxury lowrider — the most impractical vehicle in the muscle class, somehow purchasable.

vehicles.name: Buccaneer
vehicles.meta_verdict: H-tier Vapid classic at $29K — bottom of the barrel, but it costs almost nothing.

vehicles.name: Buccaneer Custom
vehicles.meta_verdict: H-tier Buccaneer with custom lowrider treatment — looks dramatically better, races no differently.

vehicles.name: Buffalo EVX
vehicles.meta_verdict: A-tier electric Bravado that actually works on the racetrack — muscle class's unlikely EV success story.

vehicles.name: Buffalo STX
vehicles.unlock_condition_note: Purchase unrestricted, but Imani Tech upgrades AND trade price ($1,612,500) both require owning an Agency — doubly Agency-dependent.
vehicles.meta_verdict: S-tier Bravado with Imani Tech gadgets — the best car in the class and it's not close.

vehicles.name: Burger Shot Stallion
vehicles.meta_verdict: F-tier Stallion with fast food branding — the worst of both worlds in one vehicle.

vehicles.name: Chino
vehicles.meta_verdict: L-tier Chino lowrider — pure cruise culture, zero race culture.

vehicles.name: Chino Custom
vehicles.meta_verdict: L-tier Chino with the Custom kit — more style, same slow.

vehicles.name: Clique
vehicles.meta_verdict: Removed SAM 2023. B-tier lowrider muscle — solid before removal, a gap that won't be filled.

vehicles.name: Clique Wagon
vehicles.meta_verdict: M-tier station wagon lowrider at $1.205M — genuinely one of GTA's most absurd purchases.

vehicles.name: Coquette BlackFin
vehicles.meta_verdict: Removed SAM 2023. D-tier Coquette oddly filed under Muscle — misclassified and correctly removed.

vehicles.name: Deviant
vehicles.meta_verdict: Removed SAM 2023. G-tier Vapid muscle — aggressive styling with limited follow-through on track.

vehicles.name: Dominator
vehicles.meta_verdict: The base Dominator at $35K is C-tier bargain muscle — functional, cheap, nothing special.

vehicles.name: Dominator ASP
vehicles.unlock_condition_note: Purchase unrestricted. Trade price ($1,331,250) requires LS Car Meet Reputation points.
vehicles.meta_verdict: A-tier Dominator variant slotting just below the GT — still elite muscle class racing.

vehicles.name: Dominator FX
vehicles.meta_verdict: S-tier Dominator variant with event livery — competes at the top with style to match.

vehicles.name: Dominator GT
vehicles.meta_verdict: S-tier Dominator that finally earned the nameplate — top muscle class pick for serious racers.

vehicles.name: Dominator GTT
vehicles.meta_verdict: B-tier Dominator with extra attitude — strong mid-tier buy at $1.22M.

vehicles.name: Dominator GTX
vehicles.meta_verdict: C-tier Dominator variant — decent filler, overshadowed by its better siblings above and below.

vehicles.name: Drift Yosemite
vehicles.meta_verdict: A-tier drift-spec Yosemite that's genuinely fast even in standard racing — surprisingly competitive.

vehicles.name: Duke O'Death
vehicles.meta_verdict: Free for returning players and better than most $2M muscle cars.
vehicles.meta_note: Free for returning players. Bulletproof cabin, great for early grinding. Best free armored car.

vehicles.name: Dukes
vehicles.meta_verdict: D-tier Charger tribute at $62K — slow, old, nostalgic, and somehow still on the lot.

vehicles.name: Ellie
vehicles.meta_verdict: H-tier muscle coupe with retro charm — a beautiful car that races like an H-tier car.

vehicles.name: Eudora
vehicles.meta_verdict: J-tier luxury muscle sedan — looks expensive, drives expensively slow.

vehicles.name: Faction
vehicles.meta_verdict: I-tier base Faction at $36K — buy it only as the stepping stone to the Custom.

vehicles.name: Faction Custom 1
vehicles.meta_verdict: H-tier Faction Custom — the lowrider aesthetic is the entire point; racing is an afterthought.

vehicles.name: Faction Custom Donk
vehicles.meta_verdict: I-tier Donk-spec Faction — the most eye-catching vehicle in the class, the worst racer.

vehicles.name: Future Shock Dominator
vehicles.meta_verdict: Futuristic Arena Dominator with sci-fi kit — same closed ecosystem as every Arena variant.

vehicles.name: Future Shock Impaler
vehicles.meta_verdict: Futuristic Arena Impaler — cool concept locked behind Arena Points and dedicated workshop space.

vehicles.name: Future Shock Imperator
vehicles.meta_verdict: Futuristic Arena Imperator — the tech-forward muscle pick for Arena sci-fi fans.

vehicles.name: Future Shock Slamvan
vehicles.meta_verdict: Futuristic Arena Slamvan — weaponized van with a science fiction makeover, Arena-only.

vehicles.name: Gauntlet
vehicles.meta_verdict: Removed SAM 2023. G-tier classic Gauntlet — the muscle class OG, long past its competitive prime.

vehicles.name: Gauntlet Classic
vehicles.meta_verdict: G-tier Gauntlet in retro trim — the classic aesthetic is real, the racing potential isn't.

vehicles.name: Gauntlet Classic Custom
vehicles.meta_verdict: E-tier Classic Gauntlet with the Custom treatment — old muscle given a fresh but slow life.

vehicles.name: Gauntlet Hellfire
vehicles.meta_verdict: C-tier Gauntlet with Hellcat energy — loud and fun, not a serious race contender.

vehicles.name: Greenwood
vehicles.unlock_condition_note: Purchase unrestricted, but Imani Tech upgrades require owning an Agency. Trade price ($1,098,750) unlocks after completing "ULP - Intelligence" (Operation Paper Trail, The Contract DLC).
vehicles.meta_verdict: C-tier Imani Tech station wagon — the gadgets make it unique, the tier keeps it grounded.

vehicles.name: Hermes
vehicles.meta_verdict: Removed SAM 2023. E-tier retro custom — beautiful Art Deco design, missed by lowrider fans.

vehicles.name: Hotknife
stores: Grand Theft Auto V and Online: Expanded and Enhanced Edition
vehicles.meta_verdict: L-tier hot rod at $90K — pure aesthetics, zero lap times, no apologies.

vehicles.name: Hustler
vehicles.meta_verdict: Removed SAM 2023. G-tier hot rod — more style icon than race car, correctly categorized.

vehicles.name: Impaler
vehicles.meta_verdict: C-tier base Impaler at $331K — serviceable muscle, outclassed by its own LX variant.

vehicles.name: Impaler LX
vehicles.meta_verdict: C-tier lowrider Impaler — pays premium price for modest gains over the base model.

vehicles.name: Impaler SZ
vehicles.meta_verdict: B-tier Impaler variant with lowrider stance — competitive enough without reaching the top shelf.

vehicles.name: Lost Slamvan
vehicles.meta_verdict: L-tier Lost MC Slamvan with no listed price — special acquisition only, race value to match.

vehicles.name: Lurcher
vehicles.meta_verdict: H-tier Lurcher hearse — Halloween aesthetic with lap times to match the horror.

vehicles.name: Manana Custom
vehicles.meta_verdict: J-tier Manana Custom lowrider — the lowrider scene's most overlooked entry, races accordingly.

vehicles.name: Moonbeam
vehicles.meta_verdict: M-tier base Moonbeam minivan at $32.5K — a minivan, full stop, nothing more to say.

vehicles.name: Moonbeam Custom
vehicles.meta_verdict: I-tier Moonbeam minivan Custom — a minivan in the muscle class is peak GTA absurdity.

vehicles.name: Nightmare Dominator
vehicles.meta_verdict: Nightmare Arena Dominator — skull-faced horror muscle locked in the Arena ecosystem.

vehicles.name: Nightmare Impaler
vehicles.meta_verdict: Nightmare Arena Impaler with monster truck energy — bone-covered spectacle over substance.

vehicles.name: Nightmare Imperator
vehicles.meta_verdict: Nightmare Arena Imperator — the spookiest muscle car you can't meaningfully use outside Arena.

vehicles.name: Nightmare Slamvan
vehicles.meta_verdict: Nightmare Arena Slamvan — bone-festooned weaponized van that never leaves the Arena War circuit.

vehicles.name: Nightshade
vehicles.meta_verdict: H-tier Vapid muscle coupe — neat design that sits at the bottom of relevant racing.

vehicles.name: Peyote Gasser
vehicles.meta_verdict: Removed SAM 2023. H-tier retro hot rod — cult following, limited pace, no longer sold.

vehicles.name: Phoenix
vehicles.meta_verdict: G-tier Phoenix with no listed price — iconic GTA nameplate running on legacy fumes.

vehicles.name: Picador
vehicles.meta_verdict: Removed SAM 2023. I-tier Picador ute — a curiosity that few mourned, taken by SAM anyway.

vehicles.name: Pisswasser Dominator
vehicles.meta_verdict: Special-livery B-tier Dominator — same race performance as the GTT with sponsored beer branding.

vehicles.name: Rat-Loader
vehicles.meta_verdict: Removed SAM 2023. O-tier Rat-Loader ratrod — aesthetic outlived its store listing.

vehicles.name: Rat-Truck
vehicles.meta_verdict: O-tier rat rod at $37.5K — the very bottom of Muscle class, still somehow loveable.

vehicles.name: Redwood Gauntlet
vehicles.meta_verdict: F-tier Gauntlet in racing livery that lied about its ambitions on track.

vehicles.name: Ruiner
vehicles.meta_verdict: Removed SAM 2023. D-tier Firebird homage — iconic hood art, minimal racing merit, now gone.

vehicles.name: Ruiner 2000
vehicles.meta_verdict: Bond car cosplay at $5.7M; the missions it unlocks don't come close to justifying it.
vehicles.meta_note: Cool concept but impractical for grinding. Outclassed by Oppressor Mk II in every measurable way.

vehicles.name: Ruiner ZZ-8
vehicles.meta_verdict: B-tier Ruiner revival with actual racing legs — the original Ruiner's long-overdue redemption arc.

vehicles.name: Sabre Turbo
vehicles.meta_verdict: D-tier base Sabre at $15K — the Custom version is worth it, this one isn't.

vehicles.name: Sabre Turbo Custom
vehicles.meta_verdict: B-tier classic muscle with the Custom treatment — old school with just enough modern pace.

vehicles.name: Slamvan
vehicles.meta_verdict: O-tier base Slamvan — buy this only as the stepping stone to the Slamvan Custom.

vehicles.name: Slamvan Custom
vehicles.meta_verdict: E-tier Slamvan with custom stance — more show than go, but the show is genuinely good.

vehicles.name: Stallion
vehicles.meta_verdict: G-tier Stallion at $71K — cheap muscle class entry that delivers exactly what it costs.

vehicles.name: Tahoma Coupe
vehicles.meta_verdict: F-tier muscle coupe that tries hard aesthetically and races like it's not trying at all.

vehicles.name: Tampa
vehicles.meta_verdict: F-tier base Tampa for $375K — save those dollars for the Weaponized version instead.

vehicles.name: Tampa GT
vehicles.meta_verdict: E-tier Tampa variant at $1.31M — the GT suffix promises more than the racing tier delivers.

vehicles.name: Tulip
vehicles.meta_verdict: Removed SAM 2023. E-tier Declasse muscle — punchy retro styling the SAM cull took too soon.

vehicles.name: Tulip M-100
vehicles.meta_verdict: F-tier Tulip variant at $1.658M — overpriced for F-tier performance, style doesn't justify the cost.

vehicles.name: Vamos
vehicles.meta_verdict: Removed SAM 2023. E-tier Declasse muscle coupe — clean retro design that ended with the update.

vehicles.name: Vigero
vehicles.meta_verdict: Removed SAM 2023. H-tier classic Vigero — the ZX's ancestor, retired alongside the update.

vehicles.name: Vigero ZX
vehicles.meta_verdict: A-tier Camaro tribute that reinvented the Muscle class — the Dominator's fiercest rival.

vehicles.name: Vigero ZX Convertible
vehicles.meta_verdict: Same A-tier performance as the ZX with the roof gone — equally competitive, twice as stylish.

vehicles.name: Virgo
vehicles.meta_verdict: K-tier classic lowrider — deep in the bottom tier but genuinely rich in character.

vehicles.name: Virgo Classic
vehicles.meta_verdict: N-tier Virgo Classic — a rolling piece of cruising culture with zero racing ambition.

vehicles.name: Virgo Classic Custom
vehicles.meta_verdict: I-tier Virgo with Custom treatment — luxury liner with I-tier racing credentials to match.

vehicles.name: Voodoo
vehicles.meta_verdict: N-tier lowrider at $5.5K — the cheapest lowrider in the game, priced exactly right.

vehicles.name: Voodoo Custom
vehicles.meta_verdict: N-tier Voodoo with Custom kit — still N-tier, now with hydraulics and style.

vehicles.name: Weaponized Tampa
vehicles.meta_verdict: Four-barrel minigun on a muscle car; fun in missions, awkward everywhere else.
vehicles.meta_note: Multiple weapon hardpoints. Good support vehicle for missions.

vehicles.name: Weevil Custom
vehicles.meta_verdict: A-tier VW Bug tribute in the Muscle class — unexpected, delightful, and genuinely fast.

vehicles.name: Yosemite
vehicles.meta_verdict: A-tier Karin pickup at $485K — best value proposition in the entire muscle class.

vehicles.name: Apocalypse Bruiser
vehicles.meta_verdict: Arena-only Bruiser with post-apoc weaponry — gated behind Arena Points and the Workshop.

vehicles.name: Apocalypse Brutus
vehicles.meta_verdict: Arena-only Brutus with destruction-spec armaments — fully locked in the Arena ecosystem.

vehicles.name: Apocalypse Sasquatch
vehicles.meta_verdict: Arena-only Sasquatch monster truck — the most chaotic Arena vehicle, appropriately gated.

vehicles.name: Bifta
vehicles.meta_verdict: Removed SAM 2023. E-tier Bifta dune buggy — light, nimble, gone with the update.

vehicles.name: Blazer
vehicles.meta_verdict: Removed SAM 2023. E-tier base Blazer ATV — the cheapest off-roader that ever existed in GTA Online.

vehicles.name: Blazer Aqua
vehicles.meta_verdict: Amphibious ATV at $1.755M — crosses water where others can't; overpriced for what it actually does.

vehicles.name: Blazer Lifeguard
vehicles.meta_verdict: Removed SAM 2023. N-tier Blazer Lifeguard — lifeguard-liveried ATV that served no competitive purpose.

vehicles.name: Bodhi
vehicles.meta_verdict: Removed SAM 2023. I-tier Bodhi — GTA Online's classic jeep, old and beloved before its removal.

vehicles.name: Boor
vehicles.meta_verdict: G-tier Boor at $1.28M — military jeep aesthetic with G-tier off-road credentials to match.

vehicles.name: Brawler
vehicles.meta_verdict: Removed SAM 2023. F-tier Brawler — rugged off-roader with genuine capability, taken by the SAM cull.

vehicles.name: Caracara
vehicles.meta_verdict: The standard Caracara — a reliable off-road platform before the 4x4 made it obsolete.

vehicles.name: Caracara 4x4
vehicles.meta_verdict: Best all-purpose off-roader — fast, stable, and handles every terrain type well.
vehicles.meta_note: Decent off-road vehicle for wilderness missions. Situationally useful.

vehicles.name: Desert Raid
vehicles.meta_verdict: D-tier Desert Raid at $695K — capable enough off-road, not competitive at the top.

vehicles.name: Draugur
vehicles.meta_verdict: A-tier off-roader that earns its ranking — one of the class's best competitive picks.

vehicles.name: Drift Walton L35
vehicles.meta_verdict: Drift-spec Walton L35 — off-road truck built to slide; fun niche toy with limited practical application.

vehicles.name: Dubsta 6x6
vehicles.meta_verdict: Six-wheel-drive monster that climbs anything; removed from stores but iconic.

vehicles.name: Dune Buggy
vehicles.meta_verdict: Removed SAM 2023. F-tier Dune Buggy at $20K — the OG off-road classic, quietly removed.

vehicles.name: Dune FAV
vehicles.meta_verdict: Military dune buggy — low-profile, fast, and great for open-terrain missions.

vehicles.name: Duneloader
vehicles.meta_verdict: O-tier Duneloader farm truck with no listed price — bottom of Off-Road and proud of it.

vehicles.name: Everon
vehicles.meta_verdict: New-gen off-roader from The Chop Shop that immediately earned a top-class racing spot.

vehicles.name: Firebolt ASP
vehicles.unlock_condition_note: Purchasable at SSASA on all platforms. Free alternate unlock (E&E/PS5/Series X only): complete Tier 4 Agents of Sabotage Career Progress challenges.
vehicles.meta_verdict: C-tier Firebolt ASP — high price for a mid-pack off-road result; the ASP suffix overpromises.

vehicles.name: Freecrawler
vehicles.meta_verdict: H-tier Freecrawler at $597K — solid rock crawler aesthetic, H-tier off-road reality.

vehicles.name: Future Shock Bruiser
vehicles.meta_verdict: Futuristic Arena Bruiser — sci-fi off-road monster locked behind Arena buy-in.

vehicles.name: Future Shock Brutus
vehicles.meta_verdict: Futuristic Arena Brutus — armored Arena beast with a science fiction makeover.

vehicles.name: Future Shock Sasquatch
vehicles.meta_verdict: Futuristic Arena Sasquatch — monster truck with tech upgrades, Arena ecosystem only.

vehicles.name: Hellion
vehicles.meta_verdict: Removed SAM 2023. G-tier Hellion dune buggy — capable off-roader taken before it found its niche.

vehicles.name: Hot Rod Blazer
vehicles.meta_verdict: Removed SAM 2023. E-tier Hot Rod Blazer ATV — the custom Blazer variant taken with the cull.

vehicles.name: Injection
vehicles.meta_verdict: Removed SAM 2023. I-tier Injection buggy — the starter off-roader that barely made it to retirement.

vehicles.name: Insurgent
vehicles.meta_verdict: The base Insurgent is I-tier and unseated — the Pick-Up Custom is always the right call.

vehicles.name: Insurgent Pick-Up
vehicles.meta_verdict: The base Insurgent Pick-Up is functional armed transport — upgrade to the Custom for real capability.

vehicles.name: Insurgent Pick-Up Custom
vehicles.meta_verdict: The Kuruma's bigger, meaner cousin — absorbs everything and keeps moving.
vehicles.meta_note: Weaponized, heavily armored, team-friendly. Excellent for co-op sell missions.

vehicles.name: Kalahari
vehicles.meta_verdict: Removed SAM 2023. M-tier Kalahari — basic rally buggy that never distinguished itself before removal.

vehicles.name: Kamacho
vehicles.meta_verdict: Top-tier off-road racer with exceptional handling on dirt and gravel.

vehicles.name: Marshall
vehicles.meta_verdict: The monster truck — drives over everything, including common sense.

vehicles.name: Menacer
vehicles.meta_verdict: Armored off-road with a gun; a capable poor man's Nightshark for budget players.

vehicles.name: Monster
vehicles.meta_verdict: L-tier Monster truck with no listed price — special acquisition that wins at spectacle and nothing else.

vehicles.name: MonstroCiti
vehicles.meta_verdict: C-tier lifted truck that delivers genuine off-road capability without breaking into the top tier.

vehicles.name: Nightmare Bruiser
vehicles.meta_verdict: Nightmare Arena Bruiser — bone-covered off-road monster that never leaves the Arena circuit.

vehicles.name: Nightmare Brutus
vehicles.meta_verdict: Nightmare Arena Brutus — horror-themed Arena armored vehicle locked behind Points.

vehicles.name: Nightmare Sasquatch
vehicles.meta_verdict: Nightmare Arena Sasquatch — the spookiest monster truck in GTA, appropriately Arena-gated.

vehicles.name: Nightshark
vehicles.meta_verdict: Bulletproof and nearly missile-proof; the definitive hostile-delivery vehicle.
vehicles.meta_note: Near-invincible armored SUV with missile resistance. Top defensive vehicle for missions and sales.

vehicles.name: Outlaw
vehicles.meta_verdict: G-tier Outlaw buggy — decent off-road bones without the performance to compete at the top.

vehicles.name: Patriot Mil-Spec
vehicles.meta_verdict: K-tier military Hummer tribute at $1.71M — expensive aesthetic purchase with no competitive merit.

vehicles.name: Ramp Buggy
vehicles.meta_verdict: Ramp Buggy at $3.192M — exists solely to flip other vehicles; GTA's greatest trolling investment.

vehicles.name: Rancher XL
vehicles.meta_verdict: Removed SAM 2023. N-tier Rancher XL — old pickup truck the SAM cull caught on its way out.

vehicles.name: Ratel
vehicles.meta_verdict: C-tier armored buggy with combat potential — solid mid-range pick for mixed off-road use.

vehicles.name: RC Bandito
vehicles.unlock_condition_note: Requires owning an Arcade property.
vehicles.meta_verdict: Tiny RC racer gated behind an Arcade; fast, low, and hilariously hard to hit.

vehicles.name: Rebel
vehicles.meta_verdict: Removed SAM 2023. K-tier Rebel pickup — basic off-road truck gone quietly with the SAM cull.

vehicles.name: Riata
vehicles.meta_verdict: Removed SAM 2023. H-tier Riata — off-road pickup taken by the SAM cull without ceremony.

vehicles.name: Rusty Rebel
vehicles.meta_verdict: Removed SAM 2023. K-tier Rusty Rebel at $3K — GTA Online's cheapest off-roader, correctly priced, now gone.

vehicles.name: Sandking SWB
vehicles.meta_verdict: Removed SAM 2023. L-tier Sandking SWB — the shorter, lesser Sandking taken alongside the XL.

vehicles.name: Sandking XL
vehicles.meta_verdict: The classic overland truck; removed from stores but a GTA Online institution.

vehicles.name: Space Docker
vehicles.meta_verdict: Alien-themed Space Docker with no listed price — a collectible curiosity from GTA's stranger missions.

vehicles.name: Street Blazer
vehicles.meta_verdict: B-tier Street Blazer ATV at $81K — the best value off-road racer in the entire class.

vehicles.name: Technical
vehicles.meta_verdict: Armed Technical pickup at $1.263M — basic mounted gun transport, outclassed by its own Custom version.

vehicles.name: Technical Aqua
vehicles.meta_verdict: Amphibious Technical with mounted gun — crosses water and fights; surprisingly practical for its price.

vehicles.name: Technical Custom
vehicles.meta_verdict: The Technical Custom at $142.5K is the best bang-for-buck armed transport in the game.

vehicles.name: Terminus
vehicles.meta_verdict: A-tier performance at a premium price — the Terminus competes at the very top of Off-Road racing.

vehicles.name: Trophy Truck
vehicles.meta_verdict: D-tier Trophy Truck at $550K — the name suggests Baja racing; the tier suggests otherwise.

vehicles.name: Vagrant
vehicles.meta_verdict: A-tier Vagrant at $2.214M — expensive but genuinely the fastest off-road racer in its tier.

vehicles.name: Verus
vehicles.meta_verdict: Removed SAM 2023. N-tier Verus — off-road utility vehicle with minimal legacy before removal.

vehicles.name: Walton L35
vehicles.meta_verdict: F-tier lifted Walton at $1.67M — heavily overpriced for F-tier off-road performance.

vehicles.name: Walton L35 Stock
vehicles.meta_verdict: H-tier Stock Walton — unmodified version of the L35, slower and somehow pricier.

vehicles.name: Winky
vehicles.meta_verdict: O-tier WW2 jeep tribute at $1.1M — wildly overpriced for O-tier; a pure collector's purchase.

vehicles.name: Yosemite 1500
vehicles.meta_verdict: F-tier lifted Yosemite 1500 at $1.205M — the truck aesthetic doesn't justify the price.

vehicles.name: Yosemite Rancher
vehicles.meta_verdict: H-tier Yosemite Rancher — lifted ranch truck with H-tier capability and genuine aesthetic charm.

vehicles.name: Zhaba
vehicles.meta_verdict: O-tier amphibious vehicle at $2.4M — crosses land and water equally slowly; pure novelty.

vehicles.name: BR8
vehicles.meta_verdict: Open-wheel racing at its purest — unforgiving but blindingly fast.

vehicles.name: DR1
vehicles.meta_verdict: A-tier Formula-style single-seater — the DR1 competes with the best Open Wheel racers at its price.

vehicles.name: PR4
vehicles.meta_verdict: Fastest open-wheel car; pure racing pedigree in every single stat.

vehicles.name: R88
vehicles.meta_verdict: A-tier R88 — retro open-wheel racer with classic IndyCar aesthetics and genuine competitive credentials.

vehicles.name: Alpha-Z1
vehicles.meta_verdict: Alpha-Z1 at $2.121M — sleek unarmed civilian jet; executive transport for the airborne crowd.

vehicles.name: Atomic Blimp
vehicles.meta_verdict: Atomic Blimp at $1.19M — giant, slow, impossible to miss; GTA Online's most visible aircraft.

vehicles.name: Avenger
vehicles.unlock_condition_note: Requires owning a Facility (Doomsday Heist property).
vehicles.meta_verdict: Flying base with vehicle workshop; overkill for most, essential for Doomsday.
vehicles.meta_note: Flying base with weapon workshop. Expensive but versatile. Niche but useful for certain operations.

vehicles.name: B-11 Strikeforce
vehicles.meta_verdict: B-11 Strikeforce at $3.8M — heavily armed attack jet that trades top speed for devastating firepower.

vehicles.name: Besra
vehicles.meta_verdict: Besra at $1.15M — jet trainer turned light fighter; fast, lightly armed, great for stunt pilots.

vehicles.name: Blimp
vehicles.meta_verdict: No-price Atomic Blimp — special acquisition, equally impractical as the purchasable version.

vehicles.name: Cargo Plane
vehicles.meta_verdict: The Cargo Plane — mission-only aircraft of legendary size; not personally owned, never forgotten.

vehicles.name: Cuban 800
vehicles.meta_verdict: Cuban 800 at $240K — the cheapest plane available; surprisingly fun to fly for the price.

vehicles.name: Dodo
vehicles.meta_verdict: Dodo seaplane at $500K — GTA's iconic amphibious aircraft; lands on water, runways, and imagination.

vehicles.name: Duster
vehicles.meta_verdict: Duster crop duster at $275K — GTA Online's slowest fixed-wing aircraft; pure charm, zero practicality.

vehicles.name: Duster 300-H
vehicles.meta_verdict: Duster 300-H at $1.02M — the upgraded crop duster; still slow, now armed, somehow worth considering.

vehicles.name: F-160 Raiju
vehicles.meta_verdict: F-160 Raiju at $6.855M — GTA Online's current fastest and most capable combat jet, period.

vehicles.name: Howard NX-25
vehicles.meta_verdict: Howard NX-25 at $1.297M — twin-engine prop with bombing capability; a gentleman pilot's aircraft.

vehicles.name: Hydra
vehicles.meta_verdict: Expensive VTOL jet with a steep learning curve; Oppressor Mk II does this job better.
vehicles.meta_note: Outclassed by other aircraft for every grind purpose. High cost, low return for grinding.

vehicles.name: Jet
vehicles.meta_verdict: The basic Jet — special acquisition large civilian airliner with no direct purchase path.

vehicles.name: LF-22 Starling
vehicles.meta_verdict: LF-22 Starling at $3.657M — VTOL jet with JATO boost ability; unconventional and capable in the right hands.

vehicles.name: Luxor
vehicles.meta_verdict: Luxor private jet at $1.625M — executive transport that the Deluxe version completely outclasses in prestige.

vehicles.name: Luxor Deluxe
vehicles.meta_verdict: $10M private jet that flies exactly like the $1.1M Shamal.
vehicles.meta_note: Gold-plated status symbol. Zero grinding utility. Pure flex.

vehicles.name: Mallard
vehicles.meta_verdict: Mallard at $250K — the go-to stunt plane; nimble, cheap, and loved by aerial performers.

vehicles.name: Mammatus
vehicles.meta_verdict: The Mammatus prop plane at $300K — basic twin-engine utility aircraft; no weapons, no frills.

vehicles.name: Miljet
vehicles.meta_verdict: Miljet private jet at $1.7M — executive transport with more seats than the Luxor; practical luxury.

vehicles.name: Mogul
vehicles.meta_verdict: The Mogul at $3.125M — twin-engine WWII-style bomber with real combat bite at a fair price.

vehicles.name: Nimbus
vehicles.meta_verdict: The Nimbus at $1.9M — large cargo plane with serious payload; the civilian freight option.

vehicles.name: P-45 Nokota
vehicles.meta_verdict: P-45 Nokota at $2.653M — WWII fighter aesthetics with real dogfighting capability; a pilot's plane.

vehicles.name: P-996 LAZER
vehicles.meta_verdict: LAZER — the classic elite combat jet, stealable from Fort Zancudo, surpassed only by the Raiju.

vehicles.name: Pyro
vehicles.meta_verdict: Pyro at $4.455M — among GTA's fastest jets; agile, lethal, and genuinely fun in a dogfight.

vehicles.name: RM-10 Bombushka
vehicles.meta_verdict: Bombushka at $4.75M — massive twin-engine bomber with real payload; the heavy-handed air assault option.

vehicles.name: RO-86 Alkonost
vehicles.meta_verdict: The Alkonost at $4.35M — stealth bomber with devastating ground-attack capability; the air force's heavy hitter.

vehicles.name: Rogue
vehicles.meta_verdict: The Rogue at $1.596M — WWII fighter with missiles and bombs; excellent value for its combat capability.

vehicles.name: Seabreeze
vehicles.meta_verdict: The Seabreeze biplane at $1.13M — vintage seaplane aesthetic with actual water landing ability.

vehicles.name: Shamal
vehicles.meta_verdict: Shamal private jet at $1.15M — fast civilian jet for cross-map transit without combat ambitions.

vehicles.name: Streamer216
vehicles.meta_verdict: The Streamer216 at $2.238M — large civilian airliner you can personally own; impractical and magnificent.

vehicles.name: Titan
vehicles.meta_verdict: Titan at $2M — massive military transport; the largest plane you can personally own in GTA Online.

vehicles.name: Titan 250 D
vehicles.meta_verdict: Titan 250 D at $4.87M — the armed Titan variant; massive transport with actual defensive capability.

vehicles.name: Tula
vehicles.meta_verdict: The Tula at $4.1M — VTOL transport with mounted weapons; versatile but outgunned by dedicated combat jets.

vehicles.name: Ultralight
vehicles.meta_verdict: Ultralight at $665K — open cockpit, maximum exposure, surprising range; the most vulnerable aircraft in GTA.

vehicles.name: V-65 Molotok
vehicles.meta_verdict: The Molotok at $4.788M — WWII-era prop-jet fighter with surprising speed and nostalgia-heavy design.

vehicles.name: Velum
vehicles.meta_verdict: Velum prop plane at $450K — basic five-seater for budget civilian transport.

vehicles.name: Velum 5-Seater
vehicles.meta_verdict: Velum 5-Seater at $1.323M — the same five-seat prop plane at nearly triple the base price.

vehicles.name: Vestra
vehicles.meta_verdict: The Vestra at $950K — sleek twin-engine prop with light weapons; solid starter combat aircraft.

vehicles.name: Volatol
vehicles.meta_verdict: Volatol strategic bomber at $3.724M — hangar-required heavy bomber with serious ground-attack capability.

vehicles.name: Xero Blimp
vehicles.meta_verdict: Xero Blimp with no listed price — the advertising blimp's rival; equally impractical, equally iconic.

vehicles.name: Asea
vehicles.meta_verdict: Removed SAM 2023. G-tier Asea — the bottom-shelf Soviet-style sedan, gone without a fight.

vehicles.name: Asterope
vehicles.meta_verdict: Removed SAM 2023. E-tier Asterope — the base before the GZ, removed before the upgrade mattered.

vehicles.name: Asterope GZ
vehicles.meta_verdict: A-tier Asterope GZ at $459K — well-priced competitive sedan with genuine class-leading potential.

vehicles.name: Chavos V6
vehicles.meta_verdict: S-tier Sedan that redefines the class ceiling — the Chavos V6 is an unexpected competitive gem.

vehicles.name: Cinquemila
vehicles.meta_verdict: S-tier Cinquemila — Italian-designed sedan that races at the very top of an overlooked class.

vehicles.name: Cognoscenti
vehicles.meta_verdict: Removed SAM 2023. H-tier Cognoscenti — Rolls-Royce-style luxury sedan, gone with the update.

vehicles.name: Cognoscenti (Armored)
vehicles.meta_verdict: Removed SAM 2023. H-tier Cognoscenti Armored — bulletproof luxury gone alongside the unarmored variant.

vehicles.name: Cognoscenti 55
vehicles.meta_verdict: Removed SAM 2023. E-tier Cognoscenti 55 — the shorter Cognoscenti, taken alongside its armored sibling.

vehicles.name: Cognoscenti 55 (Armored)
vehicles.meta_verdict: Removed SAM 2023. E-tier Cognoscenti 55 Armored — bulletproof but not tier-proof; gone with the update.

vehicles.name: Deity
vehicles.meta_verdict: B-tier Deity at $1.845M — luxury sedan with B-tier credentials and a price that doesn't match.

vehicles.name: Emperor
vehicles.meta_verdict: K-tier Emperor with no listed price — the battered old sedan of GTA; special acquisition only.

vehicles.name: Fugitive
vehicles.meta_verdict: Removed SAM 2023. D-tier Fugitive — budget four-door gone with the SAM cull.

vehicles.name: Glendale
vehicles.meta_verdict: F-tier Glendale at $200K — retro American barge; buy the Custom version for actual competitiveness.

vehicles.name: Glendale Custom
vehicles.meta_verdict: B-tier Glendale Custom — the retro American sedan upgraded to genuine competitiveness.

vehicles.name: Hardy
vehicles.meta_verdict: A-tier Hardy at $1.38M — strong upper-tier sedan with a price that matches its position.

vehicles.name: Ingot
vehicles.meta_verdict: Removed SAM 2023. J-tier Ingot — GTA Online's least interesting car, taken mercifully by SAM.

vehicles.name: Intruder
vehicles.meta_verdict: Removed SAM 2023. D-tier Intruder — low-tier sedan from the early era, quietly taken.

vehicles.name: Minimus
vehicles.meta_verdict: S-tier Minimus — compact sedan punching at the top of the class; well-priced for S-tier.

vehicles.name: Premier
vehicles.meta_verdict: Removed SAM 2023. F-tier Premier — the most anonymous sedan in GTA Online history.

vehicles.name: Primo
vehicles.meta_verdict: D-tier Primo at $9K — GTA Online's cheapest available sedan; buy it for the humor.

vehicles.name: Primo Custom
vehicles.meta_verdict: D-tier Primo Custom at $400K — the lowrider version of GTA's cheapest sedan, still D-tier.

vehicles.name: Regina
vehicles.meta_verdict: Removed SAM 2023. L-tier Regina station wagon — the bottom of sedans, taken by the cull.

vehicles.name: Rhinehart
vehicles.meta_verdict: S-tier Rhinehart — luxury sedan that makes a surprisingly strong case for the Sedan class.

vehicles.name: Romero Hearse
vehicles.meta_verdict: Removed SAM 2023. J-tier Romero Hearse — GTA's funeral car parked in the sedan class, now doubly gone.

vehicles.name: Schafter
vehicles.meta_verdict: Removed SAM 2023. B-tier Schafter — the original Benefactor sedan, taken by the SAM cull.

vehicles.name: Schafter LWB (Armored)
vehicles.meta_verdict: Removed SAM 2023. B-tier Schafter LWB Armored — long-wheelbase armored sedan gone with the update.

vehicles.name: Schafter V12 (Armored)
vehicles.meta_verdict: S-tier armored Schafter V12 at $325K — bulletproof S-tier sedan at a genuinely surprising price.

vehicles.name: Sentinel XS4
vehicles.meta_verdict: Newer Sentinel variant in the Sedan class with no racing tier — upscale entry awaiting benchmarking.

vehicles.name: Stafford
vehicles.meta_verdict: Removed SAM 2023. I-tier Stafford at $1.272M — Britain's finest gone from GTA Online stores.

vehicles.name: Stanier
vehicles.meta_verdict: Removed SAM 2023. E-tier Stanier — GTA Online's iconic police sedan, retired from civilian sale.

vehicles.name: Stratum
vehicles.meta_verdict: Removed SAM 2023. F-tier Stratum — forgettable budget sedan taken by the SAM cull.

vehicles.name: Stretch
vehicles.meta_verdict: The Stretch limousine at $30K — player-purchasable limo that races like it seats twelve.

vehicles.name: Super Diamond
vehicles.meta_verdict: Removed SAM 2023. G-tier Super Diamond — stretched luxury sedan that raced like it weighed a ton.

vehicles.name: Surge
vehicles.meta_verdict: Removed SAM 2023. G-tier Surge — electric sedan concept that never found its audience.

vehicles.name: Tailgater
vehicles.meta_verdict: Removed SAM 2023. C-tier base Tailgater — the sedan that the Tailgater S elevated beyond recognition.

vehicles.name: Tailgater S
vehicles.meta_verdict: S-tier Tailgater S — the Tailgater lineage finally delivering at the top of the sedan class.

vehicles.name: Turreted Limo
vehicles.meta_verdict: Armored Turreted Limo — the most absurd entry in the Sedan class, and that's saying something.

vehicles.name: Vorschlaghammer
vehicles.meta_verdict: High-end German sedan with no racing tier benchmarked — strong aesthetic, competitive potential unknown.

vehicles.name: Warrener
vehicles.meta_verdict: Removed SAM 2023. E-tier Warrener — retro muscle-ish sedan gone with the SAM cull.

vehicles.name: Warrener HKR
vehicles.meta_verdict: E-tier Warrener HKR at $1.26M — retro-restomod Warrener; expensive for an E-tier result.

vehicles.name: Washington
vehicles.meta_verdict: Removed SAM 2023. E-tier Washington — the quintessential GTA sedan, gone after years of loyal service.

vehicles.name: Airport Bus
vehicles.meta_verdict: Shuttle service for the impatient; roleplay essential, gameplay optional.

vehicles.name: Brickade
vehicles.meta_verdict: Armored bus for protection; only combat-useful vehicle in the class.

vehicles.name: Brickade 6x6
vehicles.unlock_condition_note: Complete the full First Dose + Last Dose mission chains (12 missions total). Then purchase from The Freakshop for $2,095,000.
vehicles.meta_verdict: Six-wheel armored transport; heavy protection without speed.
vehicles.meta_note: Required for Acid Lab business, one of the best passive income sources. Essential for that grind path.

vehicles.name: Bus
vehicles.meta_verdict: City transit for the masses; mission-essential and roleplay staple.

vehicles.name: Dashound
vehicles.meta_verdict: The Greyhound-style coach bus at $525K — GTA Online's most impractical personal vehicle, somehow purchasable.

vehicles.name: Dune
vehicles.meta_verdict: Military personnel transport at $1.3M — pure utility and roleplay; no racing relevance whatsoever.

vehicles.name: Festival Bus
vehicles.meta_verdict: Party bus without the party; pure roleplay novelty.

vehicles.name: Rental Shuttle Bus
vehicles.meta_verdict: Tourist transport; niche roleplay appeal.

vehicles.name: Taxi
vehicles.meta_verdict: Yellow cab for hire; classic roleplay, functional for missions.

vehicles.name: Tour Bus
vehicles.meta_verdict: Sightseeing bus for GTA tourists; roleplay and content creation.

vehicles.name: Trashmaster
vehicles.meta_verdict: Garbage truck; waste management roleplay and occasional mission use.

vehicles.name: Wastelander
vehicles.meta_verdict: Post-apocalyptic dune buggy in police livery; oddball collector's item.

vehicles.name: 10F
vehicles.meta_verdict: E-tier Annis with a clean aesthetic but zero competitive upside.

vehicles.name: 10F Widebody
vehicles.meta_verdict: D-tier at $575K — accessible entry for players who want to race without a major investment.

vehicles.name: 300R
vehicles.meta_verdict: F-tier Italian GT that looks expensive and races expensively poorly.

vehicles.name: 8F Drafter
vehicles.meta_verdict: E-tier coupe that looks the part but races like a D student.

vehicles.name: 9F
vehicles.meta_verdict: Removed SAM 2023. E-tier 2013 Audi R8 tribute; the entire sports class passed it by.

vehicles.name: 9F Cabrio
vehicles.meta_verdict: Removed SAM 2023. Convertible 9F; same tier, same fate.

vehicles.name: Alpha
vehicles.meta_verdict: Removed SAM 2023. H-tier at $150K — cheap enough that nobody mourned it much.

vehicles.name: Apocalypse ZR380
vehicles.meta_verdict: Arena-only ZR380 variant built for spectacle over substance — Arena Points required.

vehicles.name: Banshee
vehicles.meta_verdict: The base Banshee is an H-tier relic — the 900R upgrade is what you actually want.

vehicles.name: Banshee GTS
vehicles.meta_verdict: B-tier debut from Agents of Sabotage; immediately competitive and a genuine Pariah alternative.

vehicles.name: Bestia GTS
vehicles.meta_verdict: F-tier Pegassi sports car — decent enough but overshadowed by everything above it.

vehicles.name: Blista Compact
vehicles.meta_verdict: O-tier Blista Compact — ancient by GTA Online standards, priced generously at $42K.

vehicles.name: Buffalo
vehicles.meta_verdict: Removed SAM 2023. K-tier at $35K — the sports Buffalo; cheap, cheerful, and forgotten.

vehicles.name: Buffalo S
vehicles.meta_verdict: Removed SAM 2023. K-tier track Buffalo; an enduring muscle-sports hybrid, now gone.

vehicles.name: Calico GTF
vehicles.unlock_condition_note: Purchase unrestricted, but Imani Tech upgrades (remote control, jammer, EMP) require owning an Agency ($2,010,000–$2,830,000 from Dynasty 8 Executive).
vehicles.meta_verdict: Sport class monster with Imani Tech as a bonus; best value in its class.

vehicles.name: Carbonizzare
vehicles.meta_verdict: Removed SAM 2023. Ferrari California tribute at H-tier; soft-top and softly forgotten.

vehicles.name: Comet
vehicles.meta_verdict: Removed SAM 2023. The original F-tier Comet; better variants exist and replaced it.

vehicles.name: Comet Retro Custom
vehicles.meta_verdict: Removed SAM 2023. G-tier Benny's Comet; overpriced even when available.

vehicles.name: Comet S2
vehicles.meta_verdict: G-tier Porsche mid-gen — outclassed by every Comet successor and most competitors.

vehicles.name: Comet S2 Cabrio
vehicles.meta_verdict: Same F-tier bones as the Comet S2 with the roof off — skip both.

vehicles.name: Comet Safari
vehicles.meta_verdict: I-tier Comet Safari at $710K — the Porsche SUV crossover of GTA Online; charming but off-pace.

vehicles.name: Comet SR
vehicles.meta_verdict: Removed SAM 2023. Was genuinely C-tier competitive — one of the better sports losses.

vehicles.name: Coquette
vehicles.meta_verdict: Removed SAM 2023. H-tier but a GTA V launch icon — the Corvette that started many careers.

vehicles.name: Coquette D10
vehicles.meta_verdict: E-tier Coquette variant — better than the base, still not worth racing seriously.

vehicles.name: Coquette D5
vehicles.meta_verdict: B-tier at $1.73M — one of the better value propositions in the competitive sports tier.

vehicles.name: Corsita
vehicles.meta_verdict: Solid C-tier mid-engined sports; reliable for competitive lobbies at a fair price.

vehicles.name: Cypher
vehicles.unlock_condition_note: Purchase unrestricted, but Imani Tech upgrades require owning an Agency.
vehicles.meta_verdict: G-tier Imani Tech sports car — the gadgets are the only reason to own it.

vehicles.name: Drift Tampa
vehicles.meta_verdict: Built for drifting not racing — fun niche toy, zero competitive use.

vehicles.name: Elegy Retro Custom
vehicles.meta_verdict: Former free Social Club reward now rarely seen — E-tier legacy piece.

vehicles.name: Elegy RH8
vehicles.meta_verdict: Free from Social Club link and still D-tier competitive; a cornerstone of the sports class for a decade.

vehicles.name: Envisage
vehicles.meta_verdict: B-tier Jaguar E-Type tribute with genuine racing credentials; the price is the only hesitation.

vehicles.name: Euros
vehicles.meta_verdict: F-tier 911 tribute — beautiful daily driver, outpaced by real competitors.

vehicles.name: Feltzer
vehicles.meta_verdict: Removed SAM 2023. E-tier Mercedes SLR — a longtime SSASA staple removed in the purge.

vehicles.name: Flash GT
stores: GTA Online: Southern San Andreas Super Sport Series
vehicles.meta_verdict: F-tier flash that never delivers; the name is the best part.

vehicles.name: Furore GT
vehicles.meta_verdict: Removed SAM 2023. K-tier at $448K — wildly overpriced for the performance even before removal.

vehicles.name: Fusilade
vehicles.meta_verdict: Removed SAM 2023. K-tier at $36K; cheap and slow, a forgotten footnote.

vehicles.name: Futo
