vehicles.meta_verdict: Removed SAM 2023. M-tier but the drift community's icon at $9K — culture over lap times.

vehicles.name: Futo GTX
vehicles.meta_verdict: N-tier Futo GTX at $1.59M — the drift culture icon that races better sideways than forward.

vehicles.name: Future Shock ZR380
vehicles.meta_verdict: Futuristic Arena ZR380 with the same story as every Arena variant — cool concept, closed ecosystem.

vehicles.name: GB200
vehicles.meta_verdict: Rare rally-spec that never found its moment — a curiosity more than a competitor.

vehicles.name: Go Go Monkey Blista
vehicles.unlock_condition_note: Free at SSASA after reaching Arena War Skill rank 20 (separate progression track from regular RP).
vehicles.meta_verdict: Free community gift that barely registers as a sports car — charming, trivially slow.

vehicles.name: Growler
vehicles.meta_verdict: Beautiful E-tier Bravado — buy it for the garage aesthetic, not the podium.

vehicles.name: Hotring Everon
vehicles.meta_verdict: Oval-spec hotring racer that dominates its niche and confuses everywhere else.

vehicles.name: Hotring Hellfire
vehicles.meta_verdict: Hotring beast that roars on the oval and has no idea what to do elsewhere.

vehicles.name: Hotring Sabre
vehicles.meta_verdict: The original hotring racer — legacy status, surpassed by every newer variant.

vehicles.name: Imorgon
vehicles.meta_verdict: Removed SAM 2023. C-tier electric with real merit; quietly one of the better removals.

vehicles.name: Issi Sport
vehicles.meta_verdict: Removed June 2023 — tiny and fast; still elite for contact missions if owned.
vehicles.meta_note: Small, fast, good for contact missions and racing. Budget-friendly grind support vehicle.

vehicles.name: Itali GTO
vehicles.meta_verdict: A-tier at $1.965M — exceptional value; one of the best racing buys in the entire sports class.

vehicles.name: Itali GTO Stinger TT
vehicles.meta_verdict: D-tier at $2.38M — you're paying for the name; the performance doesn't justify the premium.

vehicles.name: Itali RSX
vehicles.meta_verdict: Best sport class car for most tracks; the safe recommendation for any racer.

vehicles.name: Jester
vehicles.meta_verdict: Removed SAM 2023. E-tier original Jester; foundation of the nameplate, outclassed by all successors.

vehicles.name: Jester (Racecar)
vehicles.meta_verdict: Removed SAM 2023. D-tier track variant; once a popular race build, now a garage relic.

vehicles.name: Jester RR
vehicles.meta_verdict: E-tier RR without the widebody kit is still just an E-tier car.

vehicles.name: Jester RR Widebody
vehicles.meta_verdict: D-tier widebody that looks the part; slightly better than the base RR but the gap is narrow.

vehicles.name: Jugular
vehicles.meta_verdict: C-tier at $1.225M — strong value for the performance level; a sensible sports class pick.

vehicles.name: Khamelion
stores: Grand Theft Auto V and Online: Expanded and Enhanced Edition
vehicles.meta_verdict: Forgotten electric concept that never found a player base — lower-tier relic.

vehicles.name: Komoda
vehicles.meta_verdict: F-tier Obey that can't decide if it wants to be a sports car.

vehicles.name: Kuruma
vehicles.meta_verdict: The armored Kuruma is the icon — the base version is just a forgettable sports car.

vehicles.name: Kuruma (Armored)
vehicles.meta_verdict: Bulletproof windows for $525K — best early-game purchase by a wide margin.
vehicles.meta_note: Best early-game grind vehicle for contact missions; near-invincible against bullets. Essential before Nightshark.

vehicles.name: La Coureuse
vehicles.meta_verdict: B-tier French sports car with sharp handling; consistently underrated, consistently fast.

vehicles.name: Locust
vehicles.meta_verdict: Removed SAM 2023. H-tier at $1.625M — expensive and slow; no competitive loss.

vehicles.name: Lynx
vehicles.meta_verdict: Removed SAM 2023. I-tier Jaguar-alike; not mourned competitively.

vehicles.name: Massacro
vehicles.meta_verdict: Removed SAM 2023. E-tier Ferrari 458 tribute that was beloved before the meta moved on.

vehicles.name: Massacro (Racecar)
vehicles.meta_verdict: Removed SAM 2023. E-tier track variant of the Massacro; outclassed even in its era.

vehicles.name: Neo
vehicles.meta_verdict: Removed SAM 2023. Solid C-tier sports that was competitive and stylish — a real loss.

vehicles.name: Neon
vehicles.meta_verdict: Electric Pfister sports car with E-tier performance — the novelty wears off fast.

vehicles.name: Nightmare ZR380
vehicles.meta_verdict: Nightmare-liveried Arena ZR380 — the best aesthetic of the three, same Arena-only limitations.

vehicles.name: Niobe
vehicles.meta_verdict: A-tier newcomer that immediately challenged Pariah and Itali RSX at the top of the class.

vehicles.name: Omnis
vehicles.meta_verdict: Base Omnis is an off-road oddity that barely registers as a sports car.

vehicles.name: Omnis e-GT
vehicles.meta_verdict: Electric crossover experiment stuck at G-tier — impressive tech, unimpressive lap times.

vehicles.name: Panthere
vehicles.meta_verdict: Retro Ferrari Testarossa tribute — stylish mid-tier with a charm that outpaces its lap times.

vehicles.name: Paragon R
vehicles.meta_verdict: Removed SAM 2023. The purchasable base Paragon; C-tier and the one most players actually bought.

vehicles.name: Paragon R (Armored)
vehicles.unlock_condition_note: Complete all 6 Diamond Casino Story Missions as host → collected from Port of Los Santos. Also available as Lucky Wheel podium prize.
vehicles.meta_verdict: Removed SAM 2023. Armored Paragon R — bulletproof prestige that's now unobtainable.

vehicles.name: Paragon S
vehicles.meta_verdict: B-tier Enus with excellent balance; the Armored's purchasable sibling done properly.

vehicles.name: Pariah
vehicles.meta_verdict: Fastest sports car ever tested — the benchmark everything else is measured against.

vehicles.name: Penumbra
vehicles.meta_verdict: Removed SAM 2023. N-tier at $24K — the drift car that never got its moment.

vehicles.name: Penumbra FF
vehicles.meta_verdict: Front-wheel-drive F-tier sports car — wrong drivetrain, wrong tier, wrong choice.

vehicles.name: Raiden
vehicles.meta_verdict: Removed SAM 2023. F-tier electric; the Neon replaced and outperforms it.

vehicles.name: Rapid GT
vehicles.meta_verdict: Removed SAM 2023. K-tier but a beloved GTA Online staple since day one — genuinely missed.

vehicles.name: Rapid GT X
vehicles.meta_verdict: C-tier successor to the iconic Rapid GT; competent but expensive for what the tier delivers.

vehicles.name: Raptor
vehicles.meta_verdict: Removed SAM 2023. N-tier at $648K — bottom performance at a mid-tier price.

vehicles.name: Remus
vehicles.meta_verdict: G/H-tier Karin sports coupe — buy it for the looks, not the times.

vehicles.name: Revolter
vehicles.meta_verdict: Removed SAM 2023. F-tier electric sports; gone before most players noticed.

vehicles.name: RT3000
vehicles.meta_verdict: Karin's Supra tribute is actually competent — sits at E-tier where it belongs.

vehicles.name: Ruston
vehicles.meta_verdict: Removed SAM 2023. K-tier at $430K; hard to justify even when it was available.

vehicles.name: S95
vehicles.meta_verdict: Grotti sports coupe with style to spare and a tier position that disappoints.

vehicles.name: Schafter LWB
vehicles.meta_verdict: Removed SAM 2023. L-tier luxury sedan in the sports class; always an identity crisis.

vehicles.name: Schafter V12
vehicles.meta_verdict: G-tier luxury sports sedan — drives like a boardroom, not a racetrack.

vehicles.name: Schlagen GT
vehicles.meta_verdict: E-tier SLS tribute — gorgeous in the garage, forgettable on track.

vehicles.name: Schwartzer
vehicles.meta_verdict: Removed SAM 2023. M-tier BMW tribute; went quietly and largely unlamented.

vehicles.name: Sentinel Classic Widebody
vehicles.meta_verdict: D-tier JDM classic with widebody kit; style-to-performance ratio leans heavily toward style.

vehicles.name: Sentinel GTS
vehicles.meta_verdict: C-tier BMW homage with clean handling; wins races without demanding attention.

vehicles.name: Seven-70
vehicles.meta_verdict: Removed SAM 2023. F-tier Bugatti-adjacent; more show than go, not widely missed.

vehicles.name: SM722
vehicles.meta_verdict: G-tier Benefactor priced like it belongs in S-tier — overpriced disappointment.

vehicles.name: Specter
vehicles.meta_verdict: The Specter Custom is already F-tier — this base version has even less excuse.

vehicles.name: Specter Custom
vehicles.meta_verdict: Fast Benny's sport that earns its keep on quick-extract missions.
vehicles.meta_note: Fast sports car with weapons. Decent for missions requiring speed and light firepower.

vehicles.name: Sprunk Buffalo
vehicles.meta_verdict: Sprunk-branded Buffalo is a collector's flex and nothing more competitively.

vehicles.name: Streiter
vehicles.meta_verdict: Removed SAM 2023. N-tier at $500K — bottom performance, mid-tier price; no tears shed.

vehicles.name: Sugoi
vehicles.meta_verdict: Dinka's JDM throwback sits in the mid-lower tier — fun character, limited ceiling.

vehicles.name: Sultan
vehicles.meta_verdict: Base Sultan at ~$12K is the best bargain in GTA sports; just don't expect trophies.

vehicles.name: Sultan Classic
vehicles.meta_verdict: D-tier rally legend with gorgeous looks; the Sultan RS Classic is the faster buy.

vehicles.name: Sultan RS Classic
vehicles.meta_verdict: E-tier rally aesthetic with a classic soul — charming but competitively irrelevant.

vehicles.name: Surano
vehicles.meta_verdict: Removed SAM 2023. G-tier convertible; a stylish casualty of the purge.

vehicles.name: Tropos Rallye
vehicles.meta_verdict: Rally-styled relic that time forgot — zero meta relevance, decent photo subject.

vehicles.name: V-STR
vehicles.meta_verdict: F-tier Benefactor that promised AMG GT vibes and delivered mediocrity.

vehicles.name: Vectre
vehicles.unlock_condition_note: Purchase unrestricted, but Imani Tech upgrades require owning an Agency. Trade price ($1,338,750) unlocks via LS Car Meet reputation. Trade price ($1,338,750) requires LS Car Meet Reputation.
vehicles.meta_verdict: Imani Tech sports car stuck at E-tier — the gadgets don't compensate for the performance.

vehicles.name: Verlierer
vehicles.meta_verdict: Removed SAM 2023. G-tier German sports; distinctive, forgettable in every other way.

vehicles.name: Veto Classic
vehicles.meta_verdict: LSCM race-series exclusive locked to Car Meet events — nowhere else, ever.

vehicles.name: Veto Modern
vehicles.meta_verdict: Modern LSCM racer that you'll never use outside Car Meet events — niche by design.

vehicles.name: ZR350
vehicles.meta_verdict: RX-7 tribute that drifts beautifully and races adequately — more style than substance.

vehicles.name: 190z
vehicles.meta_verdict: Removed SAM 2023. F-tier 190z — JDM Datsun tribute the SAM cull took without fanfare.

vehicles.name: Ardent
vehicles.meta_verdict: A-tier Aston Martin tribute with missiles and machine guns — style and violence in one package.

vehicles.name: Astrale
vehicles.meta_verdict: Stylish Sport Classic with no racing benchmark yet — an aesthetic purchase until the data arrives.

vehicles.name: Casco
vehicles.meta_verdict: F-tier Casco at $904K — handsome mid-century design that races at the low end of the class.

vehicles.name: Cheburek
vehicles.meta_verdict: Removed SAM 2023. I-tier Cheburek — GTA's beloved Lada joke car, slow by design and proud of it.

vehicles.name: Cheetah Classic
vehicles.meta_verdict: A-tier Ferrari classic tribute that earns its price — genuine Sport Classic racing pedigree.

vehicles.name: Coquette Classic
vehicles.meta_verdict: G-tier Coquette Classic — beautiful C1 Corvette tribute that drifts well and races slowly.

vehicles.name: Coquette D1
vehicles.meta_verdict: D-tier Coquette D1 — overpriced at $1.5M, trading on the Coquette name without the performance.

vehicles.name: Deluxo
vehicles.meta_verdict: Flying DeLorean with missiles — thrilling to own, terrible value next to the Mk II.
vehicles.meta_note: Flying car with homing missiles. Expensive and outclassed by Oppressor Mk II, but fun and functional.

vehicles.name: Dynasty
vehicles.meta_verdict: Removed SAM 2023. N-tier Dynasty — Chinese-inspired luxury classic taken alongside the SAM cull.

vehicles.name: Fagaloa
vehicles.meta_verdict: Removed SAM 2023. L-tier Fagaloa — Samoan car culture representation the SAM cull removed quietly.

vehicles.name: Feltzer Classic
vehicles.meta_verdict: Removed SAM 2023. C-tier Stirling GT tribute — Mercedes 300SL homage taken before it found its audience.

vehicles.name: Fränken Stange
vehicles.meta_verdict: Removed SAM 2023. K-tier Fränken Stange — GTA's most unhinged hot rod design, a true one-of-a-kind.

vehicles.name: GT500
vehicles.meta_verdict: Removed SAM 2023. I-tier GT500 — classic GT racer styling with a tier position that belied the looks.

vehicles.name: GT750
vehicles.meta_verdict: Sport Classic with no racing tier benchmarked — buy it for the aesthetics while the data catches up.

vehicles.name: Infernus Classic
vehicles.meta_verdict: C-tier Infernus Classic — beautiful Ferrari homage that races honestly at mid-pack.

vehicles.name: Itali Classic
vehicles.meta_verdict: Pricey Itali Classic at $2.025M with no racing benchmark — Italian heritage at a steep premium.

vehicles.name: JB 700
vehicles.meta_verdict: Removed SAM 2023. H-tier JB 700 — the spy car with ejector seat, removed but never forgotten.

vehicles.name: JB 700W
vehicles.meta_verdict: Weaponized JB 700 with spy gadgets and missiles — pure Bond fantasy, no racing relevance.

vehicles.name: Jester Classic
vehicles.meta_verdict: I-tier Jester Classic — retro Japanese sports car aesthetic that races near the bottom of the class.

vehicles.name: LSCM Cheetah Classic
vehicles.meta_verdict: LSCM Cheetah Classic — A-tier performance at double the base price; Car Meet exclusivity tax.

vehicles.name: Mamba
vehicles.meta_verdict: H-tier Mamba at $995K — Cobra tribute that looks the part and races near the bottom.

vehicles.name: Manana
vehicles.meta_verdict: M-tier Manana at $10K — the cheapest Sport Classic, priced exactly right for what it offers.

vehicles.name: Michelli GT
vehicles.meta_verdict: Removed SAM 2023. I-tier Michelli GT — Italian grand touring aesthetic, mid-pack at best, gone now.

vehicles.name: Monroe
vehicles.meta_verdict: G-tier Monroe — elegance over pace; the most beautiful Sport Classic in the game.

vehicles.name: Nebula Turbo
vehicles.meta_verdict: Removed SAM 2023. K-tier Nebula Turbo — Soviet-era car design that GTA Online briefly made purchasable.

vehicles.name: Peyote
vehicles.meta_verdict: N-tier Peyote at $38K — the base before the Custom; buy it as the stepping stone.

vehicles.name: Peyote Custom
vehicles.meta_verdict: J-tier Peyote Custom — lowrider VW Bug tribute that makes no apologies for its tier position.

vehicles.name: Pigalle
vehicles.meta_verdict: Removed SAM 2023. E-tier Pigalle — avant-garde French design that never found its audience in GTA.

vehicles.name: Rapid GT Classic
vehicles.meta_verdict: Removed SAM 2023. K-tier but with iconic styling — a GTA Online visual staple since launch.

vehicles.name: Retinue
vehicles.meta_verdict: Removed SAM 2023. D-tier base Retinue — the foundation for the Mk II, gone before the upgrade culture caught on.

vehicles.name: Retinue Mk II
vehicles.meta_verdict: B-tier Retinue Mk II — meaningfully faster than the base, short of the A-tier leaders.

vehicles.name: Roosevelt
vehicles.meta_verdict: Removed SAM 2023. K-tier Roosevelt — the 1930s luxury classic, gone before it could build a legacy.

vehicles.name: Roosevelt Valor
vehicles.meta_verdict: Removed SAM 2023. J-tier Roosevelt Valor — 1930s luxury car with mounted gun, gone with the update.

vehicles.name: Savestra
vehicles.meta_verdict: Removed SAM 2023. D-tier Savestra — Alfa Romeo Spider tribute the cull took quietly.

vehicles.name: Stinger
vehicles.meta_verdict: Removed SAM 2023. I-tier Stinger — an original GTA classic that quietly exited with the SAM cull.

vehicles.name: Stinger GT
vehicles.meta_verdict: Removed SAM 2023. I-tier Stinger GT — the GT badge didn't move the tier needle before removal.

vehicles.name: Stromberg
vehicles.meta_verdict: Submersible car with torpedoes; the most creative vehicle in its class.
vehicles.meta_note: Submarine car. Outclassed by Toreador but cheaper. Situational utility.

vehicles.name: Swinger
vehicles.meta_verdict: Removed SAM 2023. C-tier Swinger — clean British roadster aesthetic, competitively mid-pack before removal.

vehicles.name: Toreador
vehicles.meta_verdict: Renders Oppressor Mk II missiles irrelevant; best counter-grief car in the game.
vehicles.meta_note: Submarine car with homing missiles and unlimited boost. Excellent all-rounder for grinding and defense.

vehicles.name: Torero
vehicles.meta_verdict: Removed SAM 2023. B-tier Pegassi Torero — Lamborghini Miura tribute taken by the cull at peak form.

vehicles.name: Tornado
vehicles.meta_verdict: O-tier Tornado at $30K — the basement of Sport Classics, cheap enough to own without guilt.

vehicles.name: Tornado Custom
vehicles.meta_verdict: N-tier Tornado Custom — lowrider classic that belongs in a car show, not on a circuit.

vehicles.name: Tornado Rat Rod
vehicles.meta_verdict: Removed SAM 2023. L-tier Tornado Rat Rod — wild custom hot rod aesthetic, taken with the rest.

vehicles.name: Turismo Classic
vehicles.meta_verdict: A-tier Ferrari 250 GTO tribute at $705K — outstanding value for the best Sport Classic racing.

vehicles.name: Uranus LozSpeed
vehicles.meta_verdict: A-tier Sport Classic with genuine racing credentials — one of the class's newer competitive additions.

vehicles.name: Viseris
vehicles.meta_verdict: Removed SAM 2023. C-tier Viseris — Lotus Esprit tribute gone with the SAM update.

vehicles.name: Z-Type
vehicles.meta_verdict: Removed SAM 2023. E-tier Z-Type at $950K — famously overpriced even when it was for sale.

vehicles.name: Zion Classic
vehicles.meta_verdict: Removed SAM 2023. D-tier Zion Classic — BMW Z8-style Sport Classic gone with the update.
Removed SAM 2023. H-tier Porsche tribute that never found its footing competitively.

vehicles.name: Adder
vehicles.meta_verdict: The 2013 prestige car; $1M and tier H make it a collector piece, not a racer.

vehicles.name: Autarch
vehicles.meta_verdict: Solid C-tier that holds its own on most tracks; not flashy, just reliable.

vehicles.name: Banshee 900R
vehicles.meta_verdict: I-tier as a super but only $565K; genuinely better compared against sports class.

vehicles.name: Bullet
vehicles.meta_verdict: Removed SAM 2023. Was the cheapest super ever at $155K; K-tier but a GTA IV icon.

vehicles.name: Champion
vehicles.unlock_condition_note: Purchase unrestricted, but Imani Tech upgrades require owning an Agency.
vehicles.meta_verdict: I-tier racing at $3.75M — the worst value ratio in the entire super class.

vehicles.name: Cheetah
vehicles.meta_verdict: Removed SAM 2023. G-tier on track, legendary in culture — a Vice City ghost.

vehicles.name: Cyclone
vehicles.meta_verdict: Removed SAM 2023. The original electric super; the Cyclone II is everything it wasn't.

vehicles.name: Cyclone II
vehicles.meta_verdict: Electric super with beautiful handling; immediate top-tier from day one.

vehicles.name: Deveste Eight
vehicles.meta_verdict: Criminally underrated super with consistent top-three lap times.

vehicles.name: Emerus
vehicles.meta_verdict: Podium super that rewards smooth driving; doesn't forgive sloppy lines.

vehicles.name: Entity MT
vehicles.meta_verdict: H-tier Overflod that disappoints on track; looks the part, rarely plays it.

vehicles.name: Entity XF
vehicles.meta_verdict: Removed SAM 2023. F-tier but one of GTA Online's most iconic supers — genuinely missed.

vehicles.name: Entity XXR
vehicles.meta_verdict: Beautiful I-tier Overflod in a B-tier price bracket — the definition of style tax.

vehicles.name: ETR1
vehicles.meta_verdict: Removed SAM 2023. Was D-tier and genuinely competitive; a quiet loss in the SAM purge.

vehicles.name: FMJ
vehicles.meta_verdict: Removed SAM 2023. F-tier; succeeded by the FMJ MK V, which is the clear upgrade.

vehicles.name: FMJ MK V
vehicles.meta_verdict: Weaponized FMJ update; niche combat super for players who want missiles and speed.

vehicles.name: Furia
vehicles.meta_verdict: E-tier that looks faster than it is — which summarizes the problem entirely.

vehicles.name: GP1
vehicles.meta_verdict: Removed SAM 2023. H-tier Progen that never lived up to what the badge implied.

vehicles.name: Ignus
vehicles.meta_verdict: Late addition to the super class that immediately earned a top-tier slot.

vehicles.name: Infernus
vehicles.meta_verdict: Removed SAM 2023. G-tier but a GTA institution since Vice City — irreplaceable nostalgia.

vehicles.name: Itali GTB
vehicles.meta_verdict: F-tier Italian GT that costs $1.2M; overpriced for the performance, underpowered for the badge.

vehicles.name: Itali GTB Custom
vehicles.meta_verdict: Benny's makes the GTB pricier but not faster enough to matter; skip both.

vehicles.name: Krieger
vehicles.meta_verdict: Best overall super car; top speed and handling combined in one package.

vehicles.name: LM87
vehicles.meta_verdict: Consistent B-tier with clean handling; reliable across every track type without drama.

vehicles.name: Luiva
vehicles.meta_verdict: Recent addition still finding its place; early data suggests solid mid-tier at best.

vehicles.name: Nero
vehicles.meta_verdict: F-tier with excellent aesthetics; the garage trophy, not the race winner.

vehicles.name: Nero Custom
vehicles.meta_verdict: Benny's Nero trades money for marginal E-tier gains; still not competitive.

vehicles.name: Osiris
vehicles.meta_verdict: Was revolutionary in 2015; E-tier a decade later tells the whole story.

vehicles.name: Penetrator
vehicles.meta_verdict: G-tier Pegassi that's slow even by super standards; name is the fastest thing about it.

vehicles.name: Pipistrello
vehicles.meta_verdict: B-tier with excellent balance; one of the better-handling supers at any price point.

vehicles.name: RE-7B
vehicles.meta_verdict: Removed SAM 2023. Was a genuine B-tier contender — one of the bigger losses in the purge.

vehicles.name: Reaper
vehicles.meta_verdict: G-tier Lambo lookalike; genuinely beautiful, genuinely disappointing on track.

vehicles.name: Rocket Voltic
vehicles.meta_verdict: Rocket-boosted stunt car — not a racer, but the most fun in its lane.

vehicles.name: S80RR
vehicles.meta_verdict: Removed SAM 2023. B-tier and consistently underrated; its removal hurt the competitive roster.

vehicles.name: SC1
vehicles.meta_verdict: Removed SAM 2023. H-tier through its entire lifespan; no great loss competitively.

vehicles.name: Scramjet
vehicles.meta_verdict: Ground combat with missiles and boost; the Oppressor Mk II for non-flyers.
vehicles.meta_note: Excellent mobility with boost and jump. Good griefing defense and fast travel on land.

vehicles.name: Sultan RS
vehicles.meta_verdict: Rally super that belongs in sports class; I-tier here, surprisingly fun there.

vehicles.name: Suzume
vehicles.meta_verdict: C-tier newcomer at $3.1M — competitive but expensive for what the tier delivers.

vehicles.name: T20
vehicles.meta_verdict: Was the 2015 benchmark super; a decade of power creep left it E-tier.

vehicles.name: Taipan
vehicles.meta_verdict: J-tier — near the absolute bottom of the super class; avoid entirely for racing.

vehicles.name: Tempesta
vehicles.meta_verdict: D-tier with decent style; outclassed in a fair fight but not embarrassing at $1.3M.

vehicles.name: Tezeract
vehicles.meta_verdict: Electric hypercar concept that underperforms on every track; style over substance.

vehicles.name: Thrax
vehicles.meta_verdict: Sharp B-tier at $2.3M — competitive without being the most expensive option in class.

vehicles.name: Tigon
vehicles.meta_verdict: Removed SAM 2023. Was a solid C-tier contender — removed before players fully appreciated it.

vehicles.name: Torero XO
vehicles.meta_verdict: C-tier racer on the iconic Torero silhouette; wins lobbies, not championships.

vehicles.name: Turismo Omaggio
vehicles.meta_verdict: A-tier racing with iconic styling — one of the few supers that fully earns its price.

vehicles.name: Turismo R
vehicles.meta_verdict: F-tier Turismo R — GTA Online's original prestige supercar, now thoroughly outpaced by the modern meta.

vehicles.name: Tyrant
vehicles.meta_verdict: E-tier Lambo-alike; consistent, and consistently mediocre.

vehicles.name: Tyrus
vehicles.meta_verdict: Removed SAM 2023. Competent E-tier Overflod; gone before anyone got attached.

vehicles.name: Vacca
vehicles.meta_verdict: Removed SAM 2023. Cheapest entry into the super class at $240K; I-tier but accessible.

vehicles.name: Vagner
vehicles.meta_verdict: B-tier cornering at $1.5M — the best value in the competitive super tier by a wide margin.

vehicles.name: Vigilante
vehicles.meta_verdict: Batman car that explodes if anyone looks at it; missiles don't compensate.
vehicles.meta_note: Fast jet-powered car with missiles but no grind utility. Pure fun vehicle.

vehicles.name: Virtue
vehicles.meta_verdict: Consistently B-tier; the straightforward choice for players who want results, not experiments.

vehicles.name: Visione
vehicles.meta_verdict: D-tier performance in a gorgeous body — buy it for the garage, not the podium.

vehicles.name: Voltic
vehicles.meta_verdict: Removed SAM 2023. J-tier electric oddity; the Cyclone II is its spiritual successor done right.

vehicles.name: Weaponized Ignus
vehicles.meta_verdict: A-tier racing performance plus weapons; the only super that does both without compromise.

vehicles.name: X-treme
vehicles.meta_verdict: Off-road capable super; unique in class but limited on tarmac where supers live.

vehicles.name: X80 Proto
vehicles.meta_verdict: The F1 car that isn't — E-tier performance and a $2.7M price that stings.

vehicles.name: XA-21
vehicles.meta_verdict: Removed SAM 2023. Was a legitimate C-tier racer — a real loss from the competitive pool.

vehicles.name: Zeno
vehicles.meta_verdict: F-tier exotic that looks the part and races average; $2.8M for the disappointment.

vehicles.name: Zentorno
vehicles.meta_verdict: The 2014 meta king — E-tier today but only $725K and a genuine GTA Online icon.

vehicles.name: Zorrusso
vehicles.meta_verdict: Removed SAM 2023. E-tier Pegassi that never justified its price; not widely mourned.

vehicles.name: Aleutian
vehicles.meta_verdict: E-tier Aleutian at $1.835M — expensive for an E-tier finish; handsome styling can't disguise the tier.

vehicles.name: Astron
vehicles.meta_verdict: A-tier Astron at $1.58M — strong upper-class SUV racer, worth the investment for competitive play.

vehicles.name: Astron Custom
vehicles.meta_verdict: A-tier Astron Custom — the upgraded variant with better performance and an appropriately higher price tag.

vehicles.name: Baller
vehicles.meta_verdict: Removed SAM 2023. K-tier base Baller — the original luxury SUV that launched an entire lineage.

vehicles.name: Baller LE
vehicles.meta_verdict: Removed SAM 2023. E-tier Baller LE — the Luxury Edition that couldn't hold its tier before removal.

vehicles.name: Baller LE (Armored)
vehicles.meta_verdict: Removed SAM 2023. F-tier Baller LE Armored — the armored variant taken alongside the base LE.

vehicles.name: Baller LE LWB
vehicles.meta_verdict: F-tier Baller LWB — long-wheelbase SUV that added length and subtracted lap time.

vehicles.name: Baller LE LWB (Armored)
vehicles.meta_verdict: F-tier Baller LWB Armored — bulletproof at the cost of any remaining racing relevance.

vehicles.name: Baller ST
vehicles.meta_verdict: B-tier Baller ST — strong competitive SUV, one step below the ST-D but still elite class.

vehicles.name: Baller ST-D
vehicles.meta_verdict: S-tier Baller that dominates SUV racing — the class benchmark and the obvious first purchase.

vehicles.name: BeeJay XL
vehicles.meta_verdict: Removed SAM 2023. K-tier BeeJay XL — oversized luxury SUV parody that raced like it looked.

vehicles.name: Castigator
vehicles.meta_verdict: S-tier Castigator joins the SUV elite — competitive from the moment it launched.

vehicles.name: Cavalcade
vehicles.meta_verdict: Removed SAM 2023. K-tier original Cavalcade — the Escalade OG, retired after years of loyal service.

vehicles.name: Cavalcade XL
vehicles.meta_verdict: C-tier Cavalcade XL — the large Escalade-style SUV that trades size for racing relevance.

vehicles.name: Contender
vehicles.meta_verdict: C-tier Contender at $250K — budget-priced SUV that over-delivers for its price point.

vehicles.name: Dorado
vehicles.meta_verdict: D-tier Dorado at $1.375M — mid-pack SUV that doesn't justify the premium price tag.

vehicles.name: Dubsta
vehicles.meta_verdict: H-tier Dubsta with no listed price — G-Wagon-style SUV with special acquisition and H-tier results.

vehicles.name: Everon RS
vehicles.meta_verdict: S-tier Everon RS — top-flight SUV racer with the performance to match its premium position.

vehicles.name: FQ 2
vehicles.meta_verdict: Removed SAM 2023. J-tier FQ 2 — budget SUV from GTA Online's early era, correctly forgotten.

vehicles.name: Granger
vehicles.meta_verdict: Removed SAM 2023. H-tier base Granger — the iconic police SUV, gone from stores after SAM.

vehicles.name: Granger 3600LX
vehicles.meta_verdict: F-tier armored Granger 3600LX at $2M — protection over performance; the Escalade of combat SUVs.

vehicles.name: Gresley
vehicles.meta_verdict: Removed SAM 2023. J-tier Gresley — mid-size SUV that left no legacy behind.

vehicles.name: Habanero
vehicles.meta_verdict: Removed SAM 2023. J-tier Habanero — spicy name, plain SUV, gone with the update.

vehicles.name: Huntley S
vehicles.meta_verdict: E-tier Huntley S — aged community favorite, still a bargain at $195K for what you get.

vehicles.name: I-Wagen
vehicles.meta_verdict: C-tier I-Wagen at $1.729M — electric SUV with actual racing credentials, just not quite A-tier ones.

vehicles.name: Issi Rally
vehicles.meta_verdict: C-tier Issi Rally — the rally-spec Issi classified as SUV, surprisingly competitive for its size.

vehicles.name: Jubilee
vehicles.unlock_condition_note: Purchase unrestricted, but Imani Tech upgrades require owning an Agency. Trade price ($1,237,500) unlocks after completing 20 Security Contracts as leader.
vehicles.meta_verdict: B-tier Jubilee — Imani Tech SUV where the gadgets justify what the tier position doesn't.

vehicles.name: Landstalker
vehicles.meta_verdict: Removed SAM 2023. J-tier Landstalker — GTA's original large SUV, retired by its own XL successor.

vehicles.name: Landstalker XL
vehicles.meta_verdict: Removed SAM 2023. I-tier Landstalker XL — large family SUV taken with the SAM cull.

vehicles.name: Mesa
vehicles.meta_verdict: Removed military Mesa with no price or tier — gone from stores, barely in the records.

vehicles.name: Novak
vehicles.meta_verdict: A-tier Novak at $608K — well-priced competitive SUV that punches at the top of its class.

vehicles.name: Patriot
vehicles.meta_verdict: Removed SAM 2023. M-tier Patriot — GTA Online's original Hummer tribute, retired after a long run.

vehicles.name: Patriot Stretch
vehicles.meta_verdict: M-tier Patriot Stretch at $611K — the stretched limo Hummer; equally slow, considerably longer.

vehicles.name: Radius
vehicles.meta_verdict: Removed SAM 2023. G-tier Radius — budget civilian SUV gone with the SAM cull, barely noticed.

vehicles.name: Rebla GTS
vehicles.meta_verdict: A-tier Rebla GTS at $1.175M — AMG GLE-style SUV that earns its racing credentials honestly.

vehicles.name: Rocoto
vehicles.meta_verdict: Removed SAM 2023. G-tier Rocoto — the Toyota RAV4 tribute that left without fanfare.

vehicles.name: Seminole
vehicles.meta_verdict: Removed SAM 2023. L-tier Seminole — the base before the Frontier, gone with both SAM and relevance.

vehicles.name: Seminole Frontier
vehicles.meta_verdict: Removed SAM 2023. I-tier Seminole Frontier — lifted SUV variant removed before it built a fanbase.

vehicles.name: Serrano
vehicles.meta_verdict: Removed SAM 2023. H-tier Serrano — compact SUV that came and went without leaving a mark.

vehicles.name: Squaddie
vehicles.meta_verdict: H-tier Squaddie at $1.13M — military-style SUV that looks tough and races accordingly.

vehicles.name: Toros
vehicles.meta_verdict: The Toros delivers S-tier SUV racing at $498K — the best value proposition in the class.

vehicles.name: Vivanite
vehicles.meta_verdict: B-tier Vivanite — competitive upper-mid SUV with a solid racing pedigree and a fair price.

vehicles.name: Woodlander
vehicles.meta_verdict: C-tier Woodlander — capable mid-range SUV that sits comfortably in the competitive middle of the class.

vehicles.name: XLS
vehicles.meta_verdict: Removed SAM 2023. G-tier XLS — the unarmored base version, outclassed before and after removal.

vehicles.name: XLS (Armored)
vehicles.meta_verdict: Removed SAM 2023. D-tier XLS Armored — bullet-resistant SUV taken by the cull, no replacement offered.

vehicles.name: Airtug
vehicles.meta_verdict: D-tier airport tug — surreally fast for what it is; a workers-only vehicle hiding legitimate racing stats.

vehicles.name: Army Trailer
vehicles.meta_verdict: Army Trailer — towed equipment carrier for military logistics; not a vehicle in any meaningful sense.

vehicles.name: Baletrailer
vehicles.meta_verdict: Baletrailer — hay transport attachment that completes the agricultural vehicle collection.

vehicles.name: Boat Trailer
vehicles.meta_verdict: Boat Trailer — towed marine transport; pairs with the Docktug for complete marina roleplay.

vehicles.name: Caddy
vehicles.meta_verdict: The Caddy golf cart — for Vinewood Hills leisure and absolutely nothing competitive.

vehicles.name: Docktug
vehicles.meta_verdict: The Docktug port tractor — specialized towing vehicle for the marina and airport crowd.

vehicles.name: Fieldmaster
vehicles.meta_verdict: C-tier tractor — the Fieldmaster is inexplicably competitive for a farm vehicle; only in GTA Online.

vehicles.name: Forklift
vehicles.meta_verdict: The Forklift — exists for warehouse missions and completionists; occasionally used to annoy other players.

vehicles.name: Graintrailer
vehicles.meta_verdict: Graintrailer — agricultural trailer that pairs with the tractor; roleplay and completionist territory only.

vehicles.name: Keitora
vehicles.meta_verdict: Keitora Japanese mini-truck at $810K — niche novelty purchase for players who appreciate the very specific joke.

vehicles.name: Lawn Mower
vehicles.meta_verdict: E-tier Lawn Mower — GTA Online's most absurd racing vehicle classification; mows grass and somehow races.

vehicles.name: Mobile Operations Center
vehicles.unlock_condition_note: Requires owning a Bunker. Also serves as the upgrade bay for Mk II weapons.
vehicles.meta_verdict: MOC at $1.225M — Gunrunning's mobile command center; research, storage, and weapon customization in one massive truck.
vehicles.meta_note: Research and weapon customization hub. Required for some vehicle upgrades but very slow.

vehicles.name: Ripley
vehicles.meta_verdict: The Ripley aircraft tow vehicle — airport ground crew special; tows planes, impresses nobody.

vehicles.name: Sadler
vehicles.meta_verdict: S-tier at $35K — the Sadler farm pickup is GTA Online's most absurd racing classification.

vehicles.name: Scrap Truck
vehicles.meta_verdict: The Scrap Truck — junkyard collector vehicle for the dedicated completionist; no other use case.

vehicles.name: Slamtruck
vehicles.meta_verdict: A-tier Slamtruck at $1.31M — the vehicle-launching ramp truck that also happens to race at A-tier.

vehicles.name: Tow Truck
vehicles.meta_verdict: Tow Truck — roadside recovery vehicle for missions and roleplay; occasionally weaponized to grief other players.

vehicles.name: Tractor
vehicles.meta_verdict: The Tractor — agricultural vehicle for farm roleplay and the truly committed completionist.

vehicles.name: Trailer
vehicles.meta_verdict: Trailer — a box you tow behind things; utility gaming at its most literal.

vehicles.name: Utility Truck
vehicles.meta_verdict: Utility Truck — maintenance vehicle for GTA Online's infrastructure; no personal use case beyond owning it.

vehicles.name: Armored Boxville
vehicles.meta_verdict: Armored Boxville at $1.3M — bulletproof delivery van that's more fortress than vehicle.

vehicles.name: Bison
vehicles.meta_verdict: Slow civilian delivery van with zero performance; pure roleplay appeal.

vehicles.name: Bobcat XL
vehicles.meta_verdict: E-tier work truck filed under Vans — buy it for the $23K price tag, nothing else.

vehicles.name: Boxville
vehicles.meta_verdict: Mail truck too massive for practicality; iconic collectible.

vehicles.name: Boxville (LSDS)
vehicles.meta_verdict: K-tier LSDS-liveried Boxville at $475K — branded delivery van that races exactly like it looks.

vehicles.name: Bugstars Burrito
vehicles.meta_verdict: C-tier Bugstars Burrito — pest control livery on a surprisingly fast van.

vehicles.name: Burrito
vehicles.meta_verdict: Cheapest van in the game and slowest too, but beloved by roleplayers.

vehicles.name: Camper
vehicles.meta_verdict: I-tier Camper with no listed price — the mobile home of GTA Online, special acquisition only.

vehicles.name: Clown Van
vehicles.meta_verdict: F-tier Clown Van with no listed price — the racing stats are not funny.

vehicles.name: Gang Burrito
vehicles.meta_verdict: Gang Burrito at $86.45K — faction-liveried van with no benchmarked racing tier; a niche acquisition.

vehicles.name: Journey
vehicles.meta_verdict: J-tier Journey RV at $15K — the slowest motorhome in Los Santos, priced accordingly.

vehicles.name: Journey II
vehicles.meta_verdict: H-tier Journey II at $790K — significantly pricier than the original, marginally faster.

vehicles.name: Minivan
vehicles.meta_verdict: Family vehicle without the family missions; pure roleplay novelty.

vehicles.name: Minivan Custom
vehicles.meta_verdict: A-tier Minivan Custom at $330K — GTA's greatest joke: a racing minivan that actually wins races.

vehicles.name: Paradise
vehicles.meta_verdict: D-tier Paradise camper van — the road trip fantasy at a price that makes no excuses.

vehicles.name: Pony
vehicles.meta_verdict: Utility truck masquerading as a van; better than it looks.

vehicles.name: Rumpo
vehicles.meta_verdict: Smallest van option; marginally more maneuverable than the rest.

vehicles.name: Rumpo Custom
vehicles.meta_verdict: S-tier at $130K — the Rumpo Custom is the Van class's open secret; buy it immediately.

vehicles.name: Speedo
vehicles.meta_verdict: Delivery van built for speed it will never achieve; pure utility.

vehicles.name: Speedo Custom
vehicles.unlock_condition_note: Bundled free with any Nightclub purchase (After Hours 2018). Not sold separately.
vehicles.meta_verdict: Comes free with your Nightclub and earns its keep every single sale mission.
vehicles.meta_note: Required for MC Cocaine/Meth/Cash sell missions. If you run MC businesses, you'll use this.

vehicles.name: Surfer
vehicles.meta_verdict: K-tier Surfer van at $11K — the beach bum van of GTA Online; slow, cheap, charming.

vehicles.name: Surfer Custom
vehicles.meta_verdict: J-tier Surfer Custom at $590K — beach van with expensive custom work and an unchanged tier.

vehicles.name: Taco Van
vehicles.meta_verdict: K-tier Taco Van — the food truck of GTA Online; slow, beloved, and somehow $345K.

vehicles.name: Youga
vehicles.meta_verdict: Hipster lifestyle van; style over substance, naturally.

vehicles.name: Youga Classic
vehicles.meta_verdict: Vintage Youga without the practicality; nostalgia is the feature.

vehicles.name: Youga Classic 4x4
vehicles.meta_verdict: B-tier 4x4 Youga that races better than any lifted van has any right to.

vehicles.name: Youga Custom
vehicles.meta_verdict: G-tier Youga Custom with no listed price — special acquisition, modest gains over the base.
